quotes.quote: We present photoionization models of the optical and IR coronal line spectrum in NGC 1068 and the Circinus galaxy. The line fluxes have been calculated using (a) a non-thermal (nuclear) continuum source and (b) the non-thermal continuum plus a UV bump due to a stellar cluster. We take into account the effect of attenuation of these continua by gas with column density 10 ^ 22 cm ^ 2 located between the nucleus and the coronal line region.

In the Circinus galaxy the coronal line emission comes from gas with electron density 10 ^ 3 cm ^ 3 illuminated entirely by attenuated, non-thermal continuum. The derived ionization parameters for both coronal line regions are very similar, but the different densities imply a higher ionizing photon flux in NGC 1068, consistent with the higher observed excitation state of the line emitting gas in that galaxy.

The Circinus galaxy is also characterized by circumnuclear starburst activity. Star formation activity has been traced by H (Marconi et al. 1994), and the Br recombination line emission peaks at radius 200 pc (Moorwood Oliva 1994).

sources.summary: The paper adapts a clumpy torus model to the Circinus Seyfert galaxy and finds it can qualitatively reproduce MIDI visibilities and high-resolution spectra better than a continuous model.

quotes.quote: From our experience with modelling the SED of the Circinus galaxy with our previously used continuous Turbulent Torus Models (see Schartmann_05 ), we adopt the size of the object used there. Furthermore, we tried to stay as close to our clumpy standard model as possible (for the parameters of the clumpy standard model, compare to Table tab:model_param ) and copied the parameters , and a_0 . The rest of the parameters were changed, in order to obtain the best possible adaptation to the data, within the investigated parameter range.

The comparison of our current clumpy Circinus model as described above and in Table tab:Cir_param (yellow stars) to interferometric observations with MIDI Tristram_07 of the Circinus galaxy (black) is shown in Fig. fig:vis_circinus . From the approximate correspondence of the model values with the data, one can see that the size of the emitting region at the two wavelengths is reproduced quite well. We also showed that these kinds of models are able to qualitatively describe the available interferometric visibility and high resolution spectroscopic data of the Circinus galaxy at the same time.

sources.summary: The paper models Circinus as a Seyfert 2 with low peak star formation, no initial massive accretion event, and a nuclear disk unlike the turbulent supernova-driven disks.

quotes.quote: For Circinus we argue below that scenario I applies, because its peak star formation rate is a factor of more than 10 lower when compared to the other starbursts. This does not imply that star formation is not occurring, just that there was no initial massive accretion event.

Using a peak star formation rate of M _ * =0.02 M _ yr ^ 1 for Circinus leads to a gas mass of the massive turbulent disk which is smaller than that of the present collisional torus. We therefore conclude that scenario II (Fig. fig:scenarios ) does not apply to this AGN. Instead, scenario I yields more appropriate results. The nuclear disk in Circinus did not experience a turbulent, supernovae and stellar wind driven Q 1 disk.

sources.summary: Suzaku observations show Circinus as an active, Compton-thick galaxy whose nuclear spectrum is dominated by reflected emission below 10 keV and transmitted emission through high column density above 13 keV.

quotes.quote: We report Suzaku observations of the active, Compton-thick Circinus galaxy. Observations were obtained with both the X-ray Imaging spectrometer (XIS) and the Hard X-ray Detector (HXD). Below 10 keV, the nuclear spectrum is dominated by radiation reflected from cold dense gas of high column density, while above 13 keV the radiation is directly transmitted nuclear emission seen through a column density of 4 10^ 24 .

After NGC 4945 and NGC 1068, Circinus galaxy is the third brightest Compton-thick AGN that allows detailed study. In this paper, we present a broad band observation of the Circinus galaxy using the Suzaku telescope. The low background and high sensitivity of Suzaku allow improved constraints on the nuclear continuum and the nature of the absorbing and reflecting matter.

sources.summary: The paper cites Circinus as a nearby AGN with disk-like and torus-like dusty components, and notes possible maser evidence for a broad bipolar outflow.

quotes.quote: Direct evidence of the existence of toroidal obscuration comes from the mid- infrared observations of Seyfert 2 galaxies, such as the prototypical Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 1068, and the closest AGN, the Circinus galaxy. Observations of NGC 1068 using VLTI reveal a multi-component, multi-temperature dusty conglomerate: an inner, relatively small ( 1 pc) and hot ( 800 K) component embedded into an outer ( 3.5 pc) component which is much colder ( T 320 K) Jaffe2004, Raban09 . In the Circinus galaxy the observed elongated, 0.4 pc in diameter component is interpreted as a disk-like structure seen almost edge-on. This disk-like structure is co-incident with that inferred from the VLBI maps of H_ 2 O maser emission Greenhill03 , being embedded into a much larger rounded component. This is interpreted as a geometrically thick torus with temperature T 300 K Tristram07 .
Such evidence may already be present in the VLBI observations of a broad bipolar outflow in Circinus galaxy in H_ 2 O maser emission Greenhill03 .

sources.summary: The paper reports Fermi LAT GeV emission from Circinus, a nearby starburst/Seyfert system, with gamma-ray luminosity exceeding expectations from star formation or lobes.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of ray emission from the Circinus galaxy using the Large Area Telescope (LAT) onboard the Fermi Gamma-ray Space Telescope . Circinus is a nearby ( 4 Mpc) starburst with a heavily obscured Seyfert-type active nucleus, bipolar radio lobes perpendicular to the spiral disk, and kpc-scale jet-like structures. Our analysis of 0.1--100 GeV events collected during 4 years of LAT observations reveals a significant ( 7.3 ) excess above the background.

We find no indications of variability or spatial extension beyond the LAT point-spread function. A power-law model used to describe the 0.1-100 GeV ray spectrum yields a flux of (18.8 5.8) 10^ 9 ph cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 and photon index 2.19 0.12 , corresponding to an isotropic ray luminosity of 3 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 . This observed ray luminosity exceeds the luminosity expected from cosmic-ray interactions in the interstellar medium and inverse Compton radiation from the radio lobes.

sources.summary: The paper revises the Circinus galaxy's intrinsic continuum polarization from polarized H-alpha measurements and places it in the intermediate inclination zone while retaining its type-2 classification.

quotes.quote: I revisit the polarization of the Circinus galaxy measured by Alexander2000 , 1.9 , using the only broad emission line detected in its polarized flux spectrum, namely H 6563 (H 4861 being only marginally detected, 2 ). The EW, estimated using a Lorentzian profile Kollatschny2012 , is about 34 . From then, the ratio between type-1 H EW ( 400 ) and EW _ H Circinus is equal to 11.8 and the resulting continuum polarization is equal to 22.4 . Interestingly, Oliva1998 have derived a similar result (25 ) using a simple, numerical model applied to their own spectropolarimetric measurement of the Circinus galaxy.

ESO 323-G077 (type-1), NGC 1365 (type-1), IRAS 13349+2438 (type-2) and the Circinus galaxy (ESO 97-G13, type-2) are in the intermediate zone between the two classifications. While the type-2 classification of the Circinus galaxy is undisputed, the cases of ESO 323-G077, NGC 1365 and IRAS 13349+2438 are more ambiguous.

sources.summary: The Circinus galaxy is used as a nearby Seyfert comparison whose interferometric torus structure and X-shaped mid-infrared morphology resemble the model outflow cone.

quotes.quote: Very detailed observations have been carried out for the brightest Seyfert galaxies NGC 1068 Jaffe_04,Poncelet_06,Raban_09,Lopez_Gonzaga_14 and the Circinus galaxy Tristram_07,Tristram_14 . In these cases, the brightness distribution could be disentangled into several components, which resemble an edge-on disc-like structure on sub-parsec scales and an almost round, fluffy or filamentary larger component, probably tracing the torus walls or dust in the torus funnel.

The mentioned X-shaped morphology at MIR wavelengths, which is mainly seen for model ER20 in Fig. fig:evolution_image (lower row) can be recovered in the observations of the Circinus galaxy analysed by Prieto_04 , see especially their multi-wavelength image (their Fig. 2). Here, the V-shaped structure marks the transition to the one-sided ionisation cone . The observed central K-band source would then correspond to our central, directly illuminated core region.

To this end, a quantitative and detailed comparison to interferometric measurements of the two nearest Seyfert galaxies NGC 1068 and the Circinus galaxy is currently not feasible, but this should be done with the next generation physical models, due to the much better constraining power compared to a sole comparison to SEDs.

sources.summary: The Circinus galaxy is treated as a bright, nearby AGN with confirmed elongated mid-infrared emission and a smaller disk-like component distinct from the large-scale structure.

quotes.quote: Elongated mid-IR emission on the parsec-scale was found previously in four of the sources in this sample: NGC 424 2012ApJ...755..149H , NGC 3783 2013ApJ...771...87H , NGC 1068 2014A&A...565A..71L and the Circinus galaxy 2014A&A...563A..82T . These are among the brightest and very well studied sources ( 30 (u,v) points) where elongation and position angle can be constrained well. Our results (Tab. table:Tparelong ) are in excellent agreement with the published ones, confirming the reliability of the detected elongations.

For NGC1068 and Circinus higher spatial resolution data show a distinct disk-like structure which contributes 20 to the total flux. This disk has a different PA than the large scale structure we model here.
In the Circinus galaxy, for example, the difference is 20-30 degrees and in this source the positions angles of both the mid-IR emission and the ionization cone are very reliably determined.

sources.summary: The paper finds Circinus has the largest Fe K/Si K line ratio in the type II AGN sample, close to clumpy-torus simulations with about five clouds along the line of sight.

quotes.quote: Compared with simulation results of clumpy tori with a half-opening angle of 60 ^ , the Circinus galaxy has a ratio of 60 , which is close to the simulation results for N=5 , where N is the average number of clumps along the line of sight. The ratios of the other sources are all below the simulation results for N=2 .

The Si line (at 1.74 keV) is adjacent to the \ line at 1.745 keV. For Circinus, Mrk 3, and NGC 1068, their HEG data are deep enough to resolve the peaks of these two lines, thus allowing the two lines to be measured simultaneously.

The measured EWs are generally larger than 0.3 keV, indicating the reflection-dominated nature of the X-ray spectra of the sample. From the measured fluxes of the Si and Fe line, we can calculate the ratio after correcting for the contribution of the \ line. The results are listed in Table 1. We see that they span a range between 5-60 . The Circinus galaxy has the largest ratio around 60, while the ratios of NGC 1068, NGC 3393, and NGC 2992 are as small as 7 .

sources.summary: The paper presents the Circinus galaxy as an ideal bright southern AGN interferometry target whose resolved nuclear mid-infrared flux separates into compact and elongated dusty components.

quotes.quote: The Circinus galaxy is the ideal target for interferometric observations from Cerro Paranal. It is relatively bright in the mid-IR, highly resolved and, due to its southern declination, offers a nearly circularly symmetric (u,v) coverage allowing to probe many different position angles at various spatial scales. Thanks to its highly resolved structure and its relatively large signal-to-noise ratio, the nuclear mid-infrared flux of the Circinus galaxy has been dissected into three distinct components (see Fig. fig:circinus ) : An elongated component of about 2 pc in size, containing the majority ( 80 ) of the flux.

It seems to trace the southern edge of the near-side (north-west) ionization cone which also shows an enhanced flux in optical images . The observed wavelength-differential (or chromatic) phases observed for the Circinus galaxy can be explained by introducing a strong gradient in the Silicate absorption feature of this component. A highly elongated component of about 1 pc full-width at half-maximum (FWHM).

sources.summary: The paper reports targeted MPIPAF H I observations of Circinus, comparing a central-beam spectrum with archival HIPASS and attributing small differences to beam shape or pointing.

quotes.quote: We present results of targeted observations of two HIPASS sources, J1413-65 (Circinus) and J1909-63A (NGC 6744) from August 3 and September 4, respectively, observed with the MPIPAF, in addition to two HIPASS sources we detected in the GAMA G23 field, J2242-30 (NGC 7361) and J2309-30 (ESO 469-G015). We obtain HIPASS spectra from the archival HIPASS data cubes from the first HIPASS data release Meyer2004 .

The Circinus galaxy, was only observed with the central MPIPAF beam providing a single spectrum. We therefore compare the MPIPAF observation with the pencil beam spectrum along the same line of sight through the galaxy from the archival HIPASS data cube, as this galaxy is resolved and not contained within a single beam. The MPIPAF and HIPASS spectra agree well in shape and flux density with the only difference in that we detect some additional flux on the high frequency end of the spectrum ( 1418.5 MHz, top panel of Figure fig:hipass_specs ). This is most likely due to a slight position offset between the nearest HIPASS data cube pixel to the MPIPAF spectrum position (HIPASS pixel position: , =213.41^ , 65.35^ , MPIPAF line of sight position: , =213.36^ , 65.31^ ).

sources.summary: The paper uses Circinus as a resolved nuclear-star-cluster comparison object and reports that including its NSC gives the low end of the TDE-rate enhancement range, a factor of six.

quotes.quote: The environments in the center of these nucleated galaxies differ significantly from those in non-nucleated galaxies. As an example, we show in Fig. fig:RhoOfr the total density profile (dashed lines) as well as the bulge/NSC (thick/thin solid lines) decomposition of the dwarf galaxy NGC 205 blue; Nguyen_18 and Circinus orange; Pechetti_19 . In the absence of NSC, the central density in the dwarf NGC 205 would be up to 4 orders of magnitude lower. Not only the enhancement is lower in Circinus, but the fraction of nucleated galaxies is lower at higher mass SanchezJanssen_19 .

For the 6 galaxies for which the NSC is resolved and the density profiles is known, we find a total enhancement of the TDE rate when including NSCs varying between 6 (Circinus) to 4800 (NGC 205), with an average at 900. The mean TDE rate for these 6 NSCs is 5 10^ 5 ^ 1 . This analysis illustrates how important it is to properly resolve NSCs to have a correct estimate of the TDE rate, as their presence/absence drastically changes the central density, changing the estimates of the TDE rate by orders of magnitude.

sources.summary: The paper includes Circinus Galaxy among gamma-ray-detected SFGs, notes marginal variability, derives ASKAP 1.4 GHz flux, and averages lobe-included and lobe-excluded measurements.

quotes.quote: These SFGs have a steady ray emission Ackermann2012, ajello2020 , with the exception of the Circinus Galaxy , that shows marginal evidence of variability Guo2019 . This favours the interpretation that their high-energy emission is associated with their stellar populations instead of jets.

For NGC 4945 and Circinus , we calculate the 1.4 GHz flux using the same method but with the new Stokes-I continuum image provided by The Rapid Square Kilometre Array Pathfinder (ASKAP) Continuum Survey https://research.csiro.au/casda/ RACS, McConnell2020 . For both galaxies we obtain an rms background of 0.3  mJy/beam, consistent with the mean value 0.25  mJy/beam reported by McConnell2020 . The nearby galaxy Circinus shows extended radio lobes Wilson2011 ; we report an average flux between the one including the lobes (1.94 Jy) and the flux excluding them (1.51 Jy) in order to be consistent with the calculated fluxes for unresolved galaxies. We note that, in these cases, the fluxes we calculate may be slightly contaminated by the emission produced by AGN jets or other outflows that may arise in SBGs.

sources.summary: The paper forecasts CTA sensitivity for Circinus Galaxy, notes its sparse current gamma-ray data leave the posterior unconstrained, and compares Poisson and source-uncertainty mock data.

quotes.quote: According to model A which typically provides hard gamma-ray spectra, we expect that CTA telescope will observe at least four sources Ambrosone:2021aaw : SMC, M82, NGC 253 and Circinus galaxy. For each of these sources, we pursue the following procedure.

The only exception is Circinus Galaxy (bottom right panel) for which the current absence of data above 10 GeV leads to a different best-fit SED for model B (dot-dashed orange line referred to with ``model B ^ '').

The only exception is Circinus for which we just, currently, have six data points making its posterior distribution quite unconstrained. In this case, we obtain similar results for the two different approaches to mock data generation. We show this in Fig. fig:circinus_dist where in the top (bottom) panel we compare the distributions of for p values (Bayes factors) for the two mock datasets.

sources.summary: The paper reports IXPE polarization of the Circinus galaxy, describing its Compton-thick Seyfert nucleus, X-ray reflection spectrum, ionization cone, starburst rings, and radio jet.

quotes.quote: We report on the Imaging X-ray Polarimetry Explorer ( IXPE ) observation of the closest and X-ray brightest Compton-thick active galactic nucleus (AGN), the Circinus galaxy. We find the source to be significantly polarized in the 2--6 keV band. From previous studies, the X-ray spectrum is known to be dominated by reflection components, both neutral (torus) and ionized (ionization cones). Our analysis indicates that the polarization degree is 28 7 per cent (at 68 per cent confidence level) for the neutral reflector, with a polarization angle of 18 5 , roughly perpendicular to the radio jet.

The Circinus galaxy is one of the closest AGN redshift z=0.001449 ; distance D= 4.2 0.8 Mpc, freeman1977 . The extranuclear activity of Circinus has been well studied also at longer wavelengths, showing a prominent [ O iii ] ionization cone marconi1994 , two starburst rings at 2 \ and 10 \ from the nucleus and references therein wilson2000 , and a radio jet has been observed as part of an overall complex extended radio structure elmouttie1998b,curran2008 .

sources.summary: The paper describes the Circinus Galaxy as a nearby Seyfert 2 spiral whose ISO-SWS aperture included circumnuclear star formation, giving starburst-like dust features with weaker feature-to-continuum ratios.

quotes.quote: Circinus: The Circinus Galaxy is a nearby spiral galaxy which shows Seyfert 2 activity (e.g. Moorwood Glass 1984, Oliva et al. 1994, Moorwood et al. 1996, Oliva et al. 1998). Due to its proximity (5 times closer than NGC 1068) it has become another template object for the study of AGNs. The AGN is surrounded by circumnuclear star-forming regions, as is often the case in Seyfert nuclei residing in spirals.

The ISO-SWS observations were centered on the active nucleus, but contrary to the observations of NGC 1068 the apertures covered a significant amount of this circumnuclear star formation. Hence, most of the dust emission features seen in the starburst templates are also found in Circinus, but with weaker line-to-continuum ratio (see the discussion in Sect. s:PAH_var ). A peculiarity of the Circinus spectrum are the very pronounced features in the 20--22 m region.

sources.summary: The paper presents parsec-scale SINFONI observations of Circinus, finding rotating molecular and ionized gas, recent nuclear star formation, and a nearby inclined spiral hosting a Seyfert 2 nucleus and circumnuclear starburst.

quotes.quote: Using the adaptive optics assisted near infrared integral field spectrometer SINFONI on the VLT, we have obtained observations of the Circinus galaxy on parsec scales. The morphologies of the H _2 (1-0) S(1) 2.12 m and Br 2.17 m emission lines are only slightly different, but their velocity maps are similar and show a gradient along the major axis of the galaxy, consistent with rotation. Since V_ rot / 1 suggests that random motions are also important, it is likely that the lines arise in a rotating spheroid or thickened disk around the AGN. Comparing the Br flux to the stellar continuum indicates that the star formation in this region began 10 ^8 yr ago.

The Circinus galaxy, at a distance of 4.2 0.8 Mpc (Freeman et al. ), is an ideal subject to study because of its proximity (1 \ = 20 pc). It is a large, highly inclined ( i=65^ ), spiral galaxy that hosts both a typical Seyfert 2 nucleus and a circumnuclear starburst.

sources.summary: The paper treats the Circinus galaxy as a bright Seyfert 2 in the INTEGRAL sample, detecting it with SPI and confirming a hard-X-ray cutoff near 50 keV.

quotes.quote: We present results on approximately one year of INTEGRAL observations of six AGN detected during the regular scans of the Galactic Plane. The sample is composed by five Seyfert 2 objects ( MCG --05--23--16 , NGC 4945 , the Circinus galaxy, NGC 6300 , ESO 103--G35 ) and the radio galaxy Centaurus A. The continuum emission of each of these sources is well represented by a highly absorbed ( N_ H >10^ 22 cm^ 2 ) power law, with average spectral index = 1.9 0.3 .

A low significance ( 2.7 in the 3--10 keV band, count rate of 0.3 0.1 s^ 1 in the 3-35 keV) for Circinus has been obtained with 17 ks of data, allowing only an estimate of the flux level, while spectral extraction between 3--35 keV has been performed for Centaurus A, thanks to a highly significant detection of 84 for an exposure of 113 ks (count rate of 3.24 0.04 s^ 1 in the 3-35 keV band). NGC 4945 , Centaurus A and Circinus have been detected by SPI, with count rates of (5.1 0.7) 10^ 3 s^ 1 (20-200 keV), (1.64 0.07) 10^ 2 s^ 1 (20-200 keV) and (3.3 0.4) 10^ 3 s^ 1 (20-100 keV), respectively and SPI spectra have been extracted for these three sources. INTEGRAL confirms the presence of a cut-off at 50 keV for the Circinus galaxy, a lower limit of 130 keV for NGC 4945 and the lack of this feature for NGC 6300, in agreement with what has been found in PDS spectra.

sources.summary: The paper reports 11.9 and 8.6 micron observations of Circinus, with inconclusive 11.9 micron resolution and a slightly resolved 8.6 micron background component.

quotes.quote: Circinus Figure circinus Observations of this nearby spiral were made at 11.9 and 8.6 . The seeing at for the 11.9 point was bad, so no statement can be made whether the central peak is resolved or not. The 8.6 does show a slightly resolved background component yet whether this is a disk or circum-nuclear emission (reported by with a radius of 1'' is not clear. The image presented here is very similar to the one presented by . Recent high resolution 8.74 and 18.33 images and flux measurements are also presented by , which also find a higher 8.74 flux, 5.5-8.4 Jy, for a range of apertures from 1'' to 5'' .

sources.summary: The paper uses the Circinus Galaxy as a nearby AGN example where optical wavefront sensing is difficult, but a compact near-infrared source can support adaptive-optics guiding.

quotes.quote: One of the clearest examples of this problem is the Circinus Galaxy. Fig. dav:ao:fig:circinus shows that at optical wavelengths there is almost nothing to use as a wavefront reference, and so AO cannot perform well. It is hard to predict what performance one should expect using an AGN itself as the wavefront reference. In such cases, one can often do much better than the seeing limit, but one should certainly not expect perfect performance every time. Fortunately, Circinus does have a bright compact near infrared source suitable for AO guiding if one has an infrared wavefront sensor like that in NACO on the VLT.

sources.summary: The paper describes Circinus as a Seyfert nucleus with clumpy, geometrically thick torus emission and highlights maser proper motions as a probe of torus-cloud morphology and kinematics.

quotes.quote: Proper motion measurements of the outflow masers, especially in Circinus, are a promising method for probing the morphology and kinematics of torus clouds.

Recent VLTI interferometric observations in the 8--13 \ wavelength range by confirm the presence of a geometrically thick, torus-like dust distribution in the nucleus of Circinus, as required by unification schemes of Seyfert galaxies. Several aspects of their data require that this torus is irregular, or clumpy, in agreement with the earlier prediction of .
In Circinus, find that the torus emission extends to R = 1 pc. The AGN bolometric luminosity is \ 8 43 erg s ^ 1 Oliva99 , so \ 0.1 pc and the outer radius of the mid-IR emission is 10 .

sources.summary: The paper presents Circinus as a Seyfert 2 core where water masers, dusty disk emission, and outflow masers reveal a dusty molecular torus region.

quotes.quote: The Circinus Seyfert 2 core provides the best glimpse of the AGN dusty/molecular component. Water masers trace both a Keplerian disk and a disk outflow Greenhill03 . Dust emission at 8--13 \ shows a disk embedded in a slightly cooler and larger, geometrically thick torus Tristram07 . The dusty disk coincides with the maser disk in both orientation and size. The outflow masers trace only parts of the torus.

sources.summary: Circinus is treated as a nearby Sy2 galaxy whose high-resolution fluxes yield an excellent clumpy-torus fit with constrained inclination, cloud number, and optical obscuration.

quotes.quote: Circinus. The Circinus galaxy is a nearby Sy2 with a prominent cone-shaped region revealed in the [ O III ] images Wilson00 . Both the accuracy of the 8 high spatial resolution flux measurements and the proximity of the galaxy make the resulting fit excellent. Water vapor megamasers Greenhill03 constrain the viewing angle to i 90 . Thus, in addition to the Gaussian prior for the Y parameter, we introduce a prior on i , a Gaussian centered in 85 , with a width of 2 We choose this value of i to allow for a narrow Gaussian distribution within the considered interval [ 0 ,90 ] . .

sources.summary: The paper presents Circinus as a nearby Seyfert 2 whose mid-infrared interferometry resolves a dense inclined dusty disk, thicker torus material, and dust in the ionisation cone.

quotes.quote: The Circinus galaxy is the closest Seyfert 2 galaxy. Because its mid-infrared emission is well resolved interferometrically, it is a prime target for detailed studies of its nuclear dust distribution. An extensive new interferometric data set was obtained for this galaxy. It shows that the dust emission comes from a very dense, disk-like structure which is surrounded by a geometrically thick, similarly warm dust distribution as well as significant amounts of warm dust within the ionisation cone.

Being the second brightest AGN in the mid-infrared (.f. \ table table_sources ) and relatively nearby, at a distance of only 4 Mpc , the Circinus galaxy is one of the prime targets for studying the nuclear dust distribution in detail with infrared interferometry.

sources.summary: The review treats Circinus as the key outflow-maser Seyfert 2, with VLBI water masers tracing a warped disk and parsec-scale nuclear outflow.

quotes.quote: Presently, however, there is only one case that has been thoroughly investigated, the nearby Seyfert 2 galaxy Circinus. VLBI maps of the water emission from this galaxy have shown that maser spots trace two different dynamic components, a warped edge-on accretion disk and a wide angle nuclear outflow up to 1 pc from the central engine. Indeed, that in the Circinus galaxy represents the first direct evidence of dusty, high-density, molecular material in a nuclear outflow, at such small scales, thus allowing detailed study of the velocity and geometry of nuclear winds ( ). At larger scales, more recently, the distribution of dust in the nuclear region of Circinus has been investigated by using interferometric observations with the MID-infrared Interferometric Instrument (MIDI) at the VLTI. They found that the dust is distributed in two components, a dense and warm disk component with a radius of 0.2 pc (where the disk-masers found by are located) and a less dense and slightly cooler geometrically-thick torus-like components up to pc scales.

sources.summary: The paper models Circinus as a Seyfert 2 with a single [O III] NLR cone, finding a good kinematic fit and disk obscuration of the southeast cone.

quotes.quote: Circinus Figure circinus shows that this is a Seyfert 2 galaxy with a single NLR cone visible in [ O III ] imaging that is nearly perpendicular to the major axis of the host disk. The modeled kinematics provide a good fit to the data, matching well to both visible outflow components. At this orientation, it is unlikely that an intersection exists between the extended host disk and the NLR. As the projected opening angle of the model NLR (91 ^ ) is close to that of the opening angle seen in available imaging (96 ^ ) and the size of the NLR in this very nearby AGN is only 50 pc, it is likely that the NLR is ionizing a dense medium in the galactic disk. The south-east cone is undetectable at optical wavelengths as it is hidden behind the heavy extinction of the galactic disk Rui00,Pri05 .

sources.summary: The paper describes Circinus as a type 2 Seyfert with polarized broad lines, dust lanes, radio counterpart, water maser, large radio lobes, and Compton-thick absorption.

quotes.quote: Circinus It was optically classified as a type 2 Seyfert galaxy oliva1994 and it shows broad lines in polarized light oliva1998 . The HST image shows dust lanes (Appendix multiimages ). ATCA observations show a radio counterpart, a water maser, and large radio lobes elmouttie1998 . Circinus is a Compton thick source bassani1999 , which in fact was observed by BeppoSAX N_H = 4.3 10^ 24 cm^ 2 , matt1999 . This galaxy was observed eight times with Chandra between 2000 and 2010, and twice with XMM -Newton in 2001 and 2014.

sources.summary: The paper compares Circinus Fe and Si fluorescent-line widths, finding higher-order HEG Fe measurements about two-thirds of first-order widths and consistent with Si-line velocities.

quotes.quote: In a recent paper Liu16b , we measured the FWHM of the Si line (at 1.74 keV) for Circinus, Mrk 3, and NGC 1068, which are 570 240 , 730 320 , and 320 280 km \ s ^ 1 , respectively. These values are 3-5 times smaller than those measured from the Fe line previously, and the estimated line-emitting regions are outside the dust sublimation radii of these AGN. It indicates that the intrinsic widths of the Fe line are likely to be over-estimated.

For Circinus, the one with the best data, the line width measured from the second and third order spectra is about 2/3 of that from the first order spectra. The corresponding FWHM velocity is similar to that obtained from the Si line in Liu16b .

sources.summary: The paper uses Circinus as a Compton-thick AGN example with fitted X-ray and high-resolution IR torus models, showing stronger emergent hard X-ray flux above 10 keV.

quotes.quote: Figure fig_sed illustrates this point, showing the \ data of the well-known CT AGN in the Circinus galaxy arevalo14 , fitted with the model, also showing the intrinsic X-ray spectrum inferred using the model parameters. The figure shows that a greater fraction of X-ray flux emerges above 10 keV in the source, than below 10 keV.

One such source in common is the CT AGN in the Circinus galaxy. Along with the \ data in Figure fig_sed , we plot the high spatial resolution IR data along with the fit using the IR torus model. The inferred accretion disk spectrum is also shown.

sources.summary: The paper finds Circinus has an unusual PAH radial slope and surface flux, attributing the reverse slope to strong extinction gradients in its nuclear environment.

quotes.quote: Circinus is an outlier both in terms of radial slope as well as absolute PAH surface flux. tristram2014 have shown that it has a strong optical depth gradient of = 27  arcsec ^ 1 in the mid-IR from the west to the east side of the nucleus on parsec scales. Such a gradient was also found by roche2006 , albeit with a value of only = 0.6  arcsec ^ 1 and on larger scales. Larger scale observations indicate that the eastern side of the galactic disc freeman1977 and a circumnuclear molecular ring curran1998,curran1999 are closer to us, so that the obscuration increases towards the nucleus see also mezcua2016 . This provides a feasible explanation for the reverse slope we observe for the PAH features in Circinus compared with the rest of our sample.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Circinus galaxy’s Compton-thick Seyfert nucleus, using Chandra Fe-K spectra to constrain its torus column density, inclination, and slightly over-solar metal abundance.

quotes.quote: We analyzed the spectral shape of the Compton shoulder around the neutral Fe-K _ line of the Compton-thick type II Seyfert nucleus of the Circinus galaxy. The characteristics of this Compton shoulder with respect to the reflected continuum and Fe-K _ line core intensity are a powerful diagnostics tool for analyzing the structure of the molecular tori, which obscure the central engine. We applied our Monte-Carlo-based X-ray reflection spectral model to the Chandra High Energy Transmission Grating data and successfully constrained the various spectral parameters independently, using only the spectral data only around the Fe-K _ emission line. The obtained column density and inclination angle are consistent with the previous observations and the Compton-thick type II Seyfert picture. In addition, we determined the metal abundance of the molecular torus for the case of the smooth and clumpy torus to be 1.75 ^ +0.19 _ 0.17 and 1.74 0.16 solar abundance, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper detects highly excited OH in Circinus, finding blueshifted absorption more consistent with outflow and unphysical rotation temperatures that imply nonthermal excitation processes.

quotes.quote: Excited OH ^2 _ 3/2 absorption is detected towards NGC 253 (J=5/2), NGC 4945 (J=9/2), and Circinus (J=9/2); the latter two represent only the third and fourth extragalactic J=9/2 detections. These lines in Circinus suggest rotation temperatures in excess of 2000 K, and thus it is likely that the populations of OH rotational states are not governed by a Boltzmann distribution. Circinus's OH lines are blueshifted from the systemic velocity by 35 km s ^ 1 , while NGC 4945's are redshifted by 100 km s ^ 1 .

In contrast, previous infrared observations of Circinus have found inflow evidence, but our absorption lines are slightly blueshifted, more consistent with outflow. Comparison of the Circinus J=9/2 data to previous J=3/2 data also yields unphysically high rotation temperatures, suggesting that nonthermal processes are involved.

sources.summary: The MAGNUM analysis uses Circinus to show a one-sided northwest kpc-scale outflow whose BPT diagnostics reveal low-ionisation line-ratio structure in the outflow but not the disc.

quotes.quote: As an example, Fig. fig:vbin shows the H disc component flux maps, superimposing the 5007 outflow component contours for Circinus and Centaurus A Centaurus A shows a strong misalignment between stars and gas ( morganti2010 ), so we consider the global systemic velocity ( v_ sys =547 km/s) as a reference for the disc gas.
In Circinus (1" 20.4 pc), the outflow is extended on North-West in one-sided and wide-angled kpc-scale cone, first revealed by marconi1994 .

The left and middle panels of Fig. fig:cir_bpt show the BPT diagrams of Circinus for the disc and outflow, respectively. The corresponding position on the outflowing gas component map is shown in the right panel of Fig. fig:cir_bpt . We noticed that the highest and lowest values of low-ionisation line ratios (LILrs; i.e. /H and /H ), displayed in dark red and orange, are prominent in the AGN/LI(N)ER-dominated outflow component, while they are not observable in the disc component.

sources.summary: The paper treats the Circinus Galaxy as a Seyfert 2 observed repeatedly by Chandra/HETG, using its ionized and fluorescence-line spectra in simultaneous fits and later noting discrepant low-Z fluorescence ratios.

quotes.quote: The Circinus galaxy is a Seyfert 2 at redshift z=0.001449 , which was observed by the Chandra HETG sixteen times between the years 2000 and 2009 for a total of 667268 s. A list of observations is given in Table tab:obs . These spectra feature both ionized and fluorescence lines and were reported by sambruna2000high and arevalo20142 . In a Seyfert 2 the X-ray source is an extended ionization cone and not expected to change between observations, thus we performed spectral fits simultaneously on all observed spectra.

Unlike the other sources, Circinus and GX 301-2 show a systematic discrepancy of all low-Z elements with theoretical values of I_f^ thick . The following sections will attempt to provide an explanation to this discrepancy.

sources.summary: The paper uses Circinus as a steep-spectrum megamaser AGN whose recalibrated X-ray scaling remains consistent and whose emission traces a warped disk plus wide-angle outflow.

quotes.quote: To test this hypothesis, we have selected the two sources with the flattest spectra of our sample (NGC 4388 and NGC 4945) and the two sources with the steepest spectra (IC 2560 and Circinus), and recalculated their \ with the scaling method assuming =2.17 for the flattest sources and =1.66 for the steepest sources.

Instead, all four sources, which were originally consistent with their maser respective estimates based on the mass ratio criterion described above (see Table table:tab5 and Figure figure:fig6 ), showed a clear departure from the dynamical \ values with three sources (NGC 4388, NGC 4945, and IC 2560) that were not formally consistent with the maser values anymore (their new mass ratios were 8.0, 7.1, and 6.7, respectively) and only Circinus (ratio of 0.5) still consistent, but only by virtue of the fact that the original ratio was basically 1.

Finally, the megamaser emission in Circinus appears to be associated with a warped accretion disk and a wide-angle outflow green03 . Circinus: A detailed analysis of the , , and \ spectra of this source was carried out by are14 . Combining the complementary properties of these observatories (i.e., the high sensitivities of \ and \ over broad energy ranges and the sub-arcsecond spatial resolution of ), the authors were able to disentangle the contributions of different contamination sources (diffuse emission from the host galaxy, supernova remnant contribution, and off-nuclear X-ray binary sources) from the AGN emission within the \ extraction region.

sources.summary: The paper presents BVRI polarimetric maps of the Circinus galaxy nucleus, modeling the obscured Seyfert 2 AGN with a compact dusty disc and polar hollow-cone structure.

quotes.quote: We present polarimetric maps of the Circinus galaxy nucleus in the BVRI bands, obtained with VLT/FORS2. Circinus is the closest Seyfert 2 galaxy and harbours an archetypal obscured active galactic nucleus (AGN). Recent high angular resolution imaging revealed that a major fraction of its mid-infrared (MIR) emission is coming from the polar region.

Previously, we demonstrated that these observations are consistent with a model of a compact dusty disc and a hyperboloid shell, resembling a hollow cone on larger scales. Here we focus on the AGN core, up to 40 pc from the central engine, and compare the observations to the radiative transfer models.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Circinus Galaxy as the most promising Southern Seyfert target because its high intrinsic X-ray flux is likely coronal and its neutrino emission would appear point-like.

quotes.quote: The two objects with the largest number of expected neutrinos are Centaurus A (Cen A) and Circinus Galaxy, with 2.5 and 1.7 neutrinos predicted, respectively, if all of our assumptions are exactly met.

As the X-ray emission from Circinus Galaxy is believed to be coronal in origin, the model prediction is more robust, thus making it the most promising source in this analysis. Due to its proximity to the Milky Way, Circinus Galaxy is, in principle, visible as an extended object. Nevertheless, neutrino emission from the coronal region of its AGN would appear as a point-like source to IceCube.

According to the disc-corona model for neutrino emission from AGN, the Circinus Galaxy is the most promising target in our search because of its high intrinsic X-ray flux that is believed to originate from the corona of the AGN.

sources.summary: The paper reports resolved mm-wave emission around the Circinus Galaxy nuclear core, including east-west extended emission and additional northwest and symmetric structures.

quotes.quote: 68: Circinus Galaxy Around the bright nuclear core ( a ), extended emission in the east-west direction ( b ) exists on a scale of a few pc. In addition, there appear to be two components: one located to the NW ( ) and the symmetrically distributed structure from north to south ( d ). In an area within a radius of 0 35 which includes all the above structures, the flux density is 30 mJy. Since the peak flux density per beam is 15 mJy beam ^ 1 , an important fraction of the mm-wave emission within the area is resolved at our resolution of 0.5 pc. Submm-wave ALMA Band-7 continuum emission was imaged at a resolution of 0 3 by .

sources.summary: The paper models Circinus with LAT upper limits, finding it consistent with corona or accretion-shock scenarios and among the best southern-sky neutrino targets.

quotes.quote: In this work, we present 20 MeV -- 1 TeV spectra of three Seyfert galaxies whose nuclei are predicted to be active in neutrinos, NGC 4151, NGC 4945 and the Circinus Galaxy, using 14.4 years of the Fermi LAT data. We use M_ BH =1.0 10^7 M_ and L_X=2.6 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 for NGC 4151 Bentz:2022aaa,Koss:2022lkb , M_ BH =1.4 10^6 M_ and L_X=3.3 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 Greenhill:1997kv,Puccetti:2014pqa for NGC 4945 , and M_ BH =1.7 10^6 M_ and L_X=10^ 42.5 erg s ^ 1 for the Circinus Galaxy Greenhill:2003bz,Tanimoto:2019tbz , respectively.

For Circinus, as shown in Figures fig:AGN1 and fig:AGN2 right, we only have upper limits at sub-GeV energies, which are also consistent with both of the corona and accretion shock models. The corona model predicts that NGC 4945 and the Circinus galaxy are Seyfert galaxies that are among the most promising for neutrino detection, and the all-flavor neutrino flux reaches E_ F_ E_ 10 ^ 8 10 ^ 7 GeV cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 as seen in Figure fig:AGN1 . NGC 1068 and NGC 4151 are the most promising neutrino sources in the IceCube sky, while NGC 4945 and Circinus are the best in the southern sky.

sources.summary: The paper presents a 60 ks HETGS X-ray study of Circinus, finding rich soft and hard emission lines and a compact reprocessing region around the obscured nucleus.

quotes.quote: Results from a 60 ks \ HETGS observation of the nearby Seyfert 2 \ are presented. The spectrum shows a wealth of emission lines at both soft and hard X-rays, including lines of Ne, Mg, Si, S, Ar, Ca, and Fe, and a prominent \ line at 6.4 keV. We identify several of the He-like components and measure several of the Lyman lines of the H-like ions. The lines' profiles are unresolved at the limited signal-to-noise ratio of the data. Our analysis of the zeroth-order image in a companion paper constrains the size of the emission region to be 20--60 pc, suggesting that emission within this volume is almost entirely due to the reprocessing of the obscured central source.

sources.summary: The paper reports that Circinus has asymmetric soft X-ray emission along the ionization cone, a plume coincident with [O III], possible wind or photoionized gas, and scattered nuclear radiation.

quotes.quote: Circinus The soft X-ray emission from Circinus has an asymmetrical extension to the northwest in the direction of the optical ionization cone. The soft X-ray ``plume'' (Fig. \ 16 and Fig. \ 12 d ) coincides with the large-scale [ O III ] emission (Smith Wilson 2001). The X-rays may be associated with a hot radiatively driven wind, shocks, or highly excited photoionized gas. Closer to the core on smaller scales ( 50 pc), soft X-rays are consistent with scattered nuclear radiation by ionized gas (Sambruna et al. \ 2001). The line emission is a a combination of highly ionized material together with a low-ionization reflector.

sources.summary: The paper describes Circinus as a dust-obscured SAb active galaxy with a one-sided ionization cone and the only resolved central K-to-M-band core in the initial AGN-Heidelberg results.

quotes.quote: Circinus is a SAb galaxy inclined by 65 . 1 arcsec 19 pc. Several dust lanes hide a large fraction of its East side, including its nucleus. It shows a well known, one side nuclear ionization cone extending on the kpc scale in the North-West direction. Circinus is the only case so far where we find a resolved central core from K- to M- bands with a size FWHM= 1.9 0.6 pc. This size could be measured accurately due to the presence of several stars in the same field.

sources.summary: The paper maps Circinus coronal-line emission, finding a 30 pc extended [SiVII]/[SiVI] region aligned with its ionization cone and possible cone-wall morphology.

quotes.quote: In Circinus, [ SiVII ] 2.48 \ emission extends across the nucleus and aligns with the orientation of its one-sided ionization cone, seen in H , or in [ OIII ] 5007 . In these lines, the counter-cone is not seen (Wilson et al. 2002), but in [ SiVII ] , presumably owing to the reduced extinction, extended diffuse emission is detected at the counter-cone position (Fig. 1f; Prieto et al. 2004). This has been further confirmed with VLT/ISAAC spectroscopy which shows both [ SiVII ] 2.48 \ and [ SiVI ] 1.96 \ extending up to 30 pc radius from the nucleus (Rodriguez-Ardila et al. 2004). In the coronal line image, the North-West emission is defining an opening cone angle larger than that in H . The morphology of [ SiVII ] in this region is suggestive of the coronal emission tracing the walls of the ionization cone (see fig. 1f).

sources.summary: The paper detects Circinus Galaxy in INTEGRAL/SPI hard X-rays inside the GRO J1411-64 error region, finds its light curve constant, and rules it out as the MeV counterpart.

quotes.quote: From the possible counterparts of GRO J1411-64 discussed in Zhang et al.'s paper (2002), only the Circinus Galaxy shows up in this error region as seen by INTEGRAL. The most significant detection of Circinus Galaxy is in the energies 20-40 keV, at a confidence level of 38 .

For SPI, the Circinus Galaxy is at the 6 level in the 20-40 keV range, and it is the only source detected within the location of GRO J1411-64, the region of our search. The light curve for the Circinus Galaxy, detected mainly by IBIS/ISGRI, is rather constant.
Circinus Galaxy can be safely ruled out as the counterpart due to its spectral extrapolation well below the ones at MeV energies.

sources.summary: The paper detects radio recombination lines from the Circinus galaxy starburst nucleus and models its ionized gas, O-star requirement, and star-formation rate.

quotes.quote: We detected for the first time RRLs from the starburst nuclei in the Circinus galaxy and NGC 1808. The Circinus galaxy was detected in RRL emission with a line strength integrated over the source of 3.2 mJy, making it the fourth-strongest extragalactic RRL emitter known at this frequency (after NGC 4945, M 82, and NGC 253) and so is suitable for detailed study. The line and continuum emission from the Circinus galaxy can be matched by a model consisting of a collection of 50 to 10 000 H II regions with temperatures of 5000 K, densities of 500 cm ^ 3 to 50 000 cm ^ 3 , and a total effective diameter of 3 pc to 50 pc. The Lyman continuum production rate required to maintain the ionization is 1 10^ 52 s ^ 1 to 3 10^ 53 s ^ 1 , which requires 300 to 9000 O5 stars to be produced in the starburst, inferring a star formation rate of 0.2 M_ yr ^ 1 to 6 M_ yr ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper excludes Circinus from the Baldwin-effect correlations, attributing its strong deviations to dust extinction toward the nucleus and a deep silicate feature.

quotes.quote: In the case of Circinus, we show that despite the use of mid-infrared lines, obscuration by either the host galaxy or the circumnuclear dust torus might affect the equivalent widths.
The case of Circinus We excluded Circinus from all analysis of the correlations.

Circinus is known for strong dust extinction towards the nucleus by dust lanes in both the host and our own Galaxy. In Fig. Fig:Obscure , we plot the deviation from the nominal correlation with X-ray luminosity (as given in Figs. Fig:LineComb Fig:NeII ) of each line against the depth of the silicate feature. Circinus shows the strongest deviations from all correlations and, at the same time, has the deepest silicate feature.

sources.summary: The paper models Circinus as a nearby Seyfert with a deep silicate feature; CLUMPY torus fits work reasonably well, likely because extended-dust contamination is reduced.

quotes.quote: For the Circinus galaxy, which has a relatively deep silicate feature ( S_ 9.7 =-1.8/-2.4 , Roche et al. 2007 and Table 4), CLUMPY torus models with the parameters given in Table 5 plus a foreground absorbing screen with A_V( frg )=9 mag reproduce reasonably well the silicate feature and the photometric SED, except for the 18.3 m flux.

One possibility for these three galaxies is that there is an important contamination from extended dust structures, while in Circinus, the closest galaxy in our sample ( d=4 Mpc) this contamination is minimized.

In contrast, we were able to fit reasonably well the data of Circinus. One possibility is that because Circinus is the closest galaxy in our sample (distance of 4 Mpc), the contamination by extended dusty structures is reduced.

sources.summary: The paper characterizes Circinus as an active starburst spiral and Seyfert 2 system similar to NGC 1068, with dense central molecular gas observed in sulfur-bearing transitions.

quotes.quote: The Circinus galaxy is an active, starburst, spiral galaxy. NGC 1068 and Circinus are similar. Both are typical Seyfert 2 galaxies close to the Milky Way and contain the molecular gas which surrounds nuclei, as well as star-forming rings Zhang2014 . Several molecular transitions, and among them in sulfur species CS and SO, were observed in the study of a dense molecular gas in the central part of the Circinus galaxy using the SEST telescope Curran2001 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies the Circinus galaxy as the reddest local Seyfert outlier, with weak hot dust emission and no interferometric evidence for hot dust despite nearly resolving the sublimation radius.

quotes.quote: In the reddest of all, the Circinus galaxy with K-N 10, the hot component seems to be intrinsically much weaker than in the other sources. This has been seen in high-resolution SED models prieto2004 and recently been confirmed by a detailed analysis of the highest resolution interferometric data tristram2014 . Although the Circinus galaxy is so nearby that the sublimation radius is nearly resolved, there is no evidence for hot dust in the interferometric data.

This is in contrast to NGC 1068, where a similar resolution is reached in infrared interferometric observation and a hot component is clearly detected lopezgonzaga2014 - consistent with the ``bluer'' color of NGC 1068 compared to the Circinus galaxy.

sources.summary: The survey re-detects the Circinus galaxy as HIZOA J1413 65, adopts a 4.2 Mpc distance for it, and notes it is significantly extended in the H I survey beam.

quotes.quote: The well-known Circinus galaxy lies within the survey area, and is re-detected here as HIZOA J1413 65. The narrow velocity width, low- mass ( M_ HI = 7.24 ) detection HIZOA J1353 58, less than 8 from Circinus, has a recessional velocity that is only about 200 \ different from the large galaxy. At the low recession velocities of these objects of only a few hundred , Hubble flow distances are very uncertain, but it seems likely this newly-detected object is a previously-unknown galaxy which is related to the Circinus galaxy. As its velocity corrected to the Local Group frame is negative, and thus a Hubble distance is undefined in this frame, we adopt a redshift-independent distance measurement to Circinus, 4.2 Mpc (Tully 2009), as the distance to both HIZOA J1413 65 and HIZOA J1353 58. The Circinus galaxy as well as four other detections (J1509 52, J1514 52, J1532 56, J1616 55) are significantly extended, that is, resolved with respect to the 15 5 beam.

sources.summary: The paper reports a >2 sigma Ni-K detection in the Circinus Galaxy, notes its clear line and highest Fe-K flux, and gives its precise Ni/Fe ratio.

quotes.quote: Ni-K line was detected with >2 level for the Circinus galaxy, Cen A, MRK 3, NGC 4388, and NGC 4151.
the Circinus Galaxy, which has the highest Fe-K line flux Fuka11b , shows a clear Ni-K line, while some other AGNs show a marginal Ni-K line as NGC 4388.

The Ni-K line fluxes of Cen A (2005), MRK 3, and the Circinus Galaxy are consistent with those in the past results of Suzaku observations Mark07,Awak08,Yang09 . The average of I_ Ni /I_ Fe is 0.066 with a variance of 0.026 for measurements of single observation. The most accurate measurement was obtained to be I_ Ni /I_ Fe =0.0486 0.0066 for the Circinus Galaxy, whose equivalent width of the Ni-K line is prominently the largest.

sources.summary: The paper uses Circinus galaxy MUSE data to test a new 3D outflow-modeling approach, showing that observed maps can be reproduced through gas clumpiness.

quotes.quote: To investigate possible degeneracies of this process, we preliminary tested it on MUSE data of Circinus galaxy, and the results are shown in Fig. fig:fig2 . They show that by choosing a wrong geometry and inclination for the outflow model, the sky-projected maps obtained from it (middle panels) do not reproduce well the observed ones (upper panels). By finding the appropriate model configuration, the observed maps can instead be very well reproduced (lower panels). This first test on Circinus MUSE data shows that the complex velocity structures observed in the maps can be accounted for only by the effect of gas clumpiness on a radial and constant velocity field, without invoking complex kinematical effects.

sources.summary: The paper uses Circinus Galaxy as the observational benchmark for a compact dusty disc plus hyperboloid dusty outflow model of AGN infrared structure.

quotes.quote: IR interferometry has the capability to provide more detail about the actual geometry of the dust structure, specifically in nearby sources such as the Circinus Galaxy. Mid-IR photometric and interferometric observations of the nucleus of this galaxy have been reproduced with a model based on a compact dusty disc and an extended dusty outflow in the form of an hyperboloid circinus,circinus2 .

We have been able to qualitatively reproduce high-angular infrared observations and radiative transfer modelling of the AGN in the Circinus Galaxy. Specifically, we replicate the hyperboloid shape of the wind proposed by circinus2 .

sources.summary: The paper finds Circinus galaxy consistent with gamma-ray data and measured star-formation rates, and predicts its core could be detected by KM3NeT.

quotes.quote: Remarkably, we find that the cores of the Small Magellanic Cloud and the Circinus galaxy are potentially observable by KM3NeT/ARCA with 6 years of observation.

For M82, NGC 253, NGC 2146, NGC 2403, Circinus galaxy, M31, M33, and SMC, we find a reasonable agreement both with the data and the measured SFR values. We emphasize that in our model all the galaxies are approximated as point-like sources with a dominant emission limited to the SBN.
Remarkably, we find that the cores of SMC and Circinus galaxy could be potentially detected by KM3NeT thanks to its higher sensitivity to the southern sky.

sources.summary: The paper presents the Circinus galaxy as a nearby AGN target for MIXIM, citing its iron K photon flux and projected pc-scale spatial-structure capability.

quotes.quote: Whereas most AGNs have been conventionally regarded as point-sources for the current x-ray astronomical satellites, high-resolution x-ray imaging with Chandra resolved the profiles of some nearby AGNs extending a few arcseconds in the iron K band (e.g., Circinus galaxy , NGC1068 , and NGC4945 ).

Finally, we concisely discuss specific requirements in the case of an observation of the Circinus galaxy as an example of candidate targets. XMM-Newton obtained the iron K photon flux from the Circinus galaxy to be 2.14 0.06 10^ 4 photons s^ 1 cm^ 2 , which is an order of magnitude higher than the CXB flux in the same energy band (if with the aforementioned parallel hole collimator). Given that an opening fraction of a mask and effective exposure time is 3.2 and 10 Ms ( 4 months), respectively, MIXIM necessitates a detector with an effective area of 30 cm^ 2 to collect 2 10^3 photons; while hundreds of sensors are required if with GMAX0505 due to its thin detection layer, it would be deployable on a single satellite if we adopt a sensor with both a high spatial resolution and high detection efficiency in the future. Hence, we consider a relatively compact imaging system with f=0.18 0.18 , d=9.6 and z=4.3 (i.e., m=9 for 6.4 x-rays); such a system would have an angular resolution of ;;0.1 , which enables us to investigate the spatial structure of the Circinus galaxy (with a distance of 4.2 Mpc ) with a pc-scale resolution.

sources.summary: The paper describes Circinus as a nearby active Compton-thick Seyfert 2 with kiloparsec outflowing gas, central star formation, a parsec-scale circumnuclear disk, spiral molecular arms, and absorption.

quotes.quote: Circinus Galaxy and environment Circinus is one of the closest known active galaxies, at a distance of 4.2 0.7 Mpc and a redshift of 0.0015 Tully2009 . In the X-ray, it is the brightest known Compton-thick Seyfert 2 galaxy Bianchi2002 . On one side, Circinus has kiloparsec scale outflowing gas seen in H and [ O III ] emission lines Veilleux1997,MullerSanchez2006 . In the central 100 pc, Esquej2014 find a star formation rate of approximately 0.1 M _ /yr, with the total for the galaxy estimated at a few M _ /yr Elmouttie1998 .

sources.summary: The paper models Circinus as a nearby Seyfert 2 galaxy whose circumnuclear starburst radiation can support a dusty wall, obscuring the inner roughly 100 pc with a few magnitudes of visual extinction.

quotes.quote: To study the distributions of dusty gas in the circumnuclear regions of the Circinus galaxy, we have analyzed the equilibrium configurations and the stability of the dusty gas in the circumnuclear regions by considering the radiation force exerted by the circumnuclear starburst ring as well as by the central radiation source, which consists mainly of a nuclear starburst. As a result, it has been found that, within the observationally inferred age of a circumnuclear starburst ring in the Circinus galaxy, the radiation force can work to build up a dusty wall which enshrouds the circumnuclear regions. The wall may contribute to the obscuration of the region inside the nuclear 100 pc in this galaxy. The visual extinction A_V of the wall could be a few mags.

sources.summary: The paper describes Circinus as a nearby starburst/Seyfert hybrid where SEST mapping indicated a molecular outflow and HCN emission is linked to star-forming cores in its molecular ring.

quotes.quote: Notably, the two nearest starburst/Seyfert hybrids, NGC 4945 and Circinus, are only observable with southern telescopes. An important observation would be to demonstrate the ability of a dusty circumnuclear torus to collimate a molecular outflow: Curran:99 argued for the existence of such an outflow in Circinus based on SEST mapping, but higher resolution observations are needed to confirm its association with the observed ionisation cone and references therein Veilleux:97 .

NGC 4945 shows a higher HCN/CO ratio, and a higher HCN/FIR (far-infrared) ratio, than Circinus despite lower gas densities ( 10^4 vs. \ 10^5 cm ^ 3 ) Curran:01 . In light of BIMA maps of the Seyfert galaxy NGC 1068 which show that most of the HCN is concentrated towards the centre, whereas CO and star formation are located in a ring 1 kpc in radius Helfer:95 , this suggests that much of the HCN emission in NGC 4945 comes from more diffuse gas associated with the dusty torus in the inner 10 pc, whereas in Circinus it is associated with star-forming cores in the 500-pc-radius molecular ring.

sources.summary: The paper reports new coronal-line detections in the Circinus galaxy, emphasizing its bright, high-excitation, narrow-line spectrum and suitability for coronal-line studies.

quotes.quote: Two more coronal lines are detected for the first time to our knowledge in the spectrum of an extragalactic source: [ CaVII ] 4.09 m and [ CaV ] 4.16 m in the spectrum of the Circinus galaxy, at wavelengths in agreement with the ones inferred by Feuchtgruber et al. ( ) from observation of the planetary nebulae NGC 6302 and NGC 7027. Like [ Si IX ] and Br , they show a distinct blue asymmetry, the blue wing extending out to 800 km/s.

The rest wavelength of [ Ca V ] is covered by our spectra for Circinus only. Detection of these lines (lower ionisation potentials 109 and 67eV) confirms again the unique suitability of the Circinus galaxy for coronal line studies due to its brightness, high excitation, and narrow line widths.

sources.summary: The paper models the Circinus galaxy as a nearby composite Seyfert 2 object, with optical-to-infrared photometry and ISO spectra constraining its AGN and starburst contributions.

quotes.quote: The Circinus galaxy was finally considered as a composite object. The Circinus galaxy This very nearby ( 4 Mpc) source displays characteristics typical of a Seyfert 2 object, like for example the very intense forbidden optical lines oliva94 and a broad component of H emission oliva98 . The photometric datapoints available for this source from the optical to the far-IR and the ISO SWS spectrum ( 2.5 to 45 ; moorwood96 , see Fig. fig:circinus ) strongly constrain the AGN contribution.

For three well-known nearby active galaxies (Circinus, NGC 1068 and Mrk 231), the ISO mid-IR spectrum was also available, in which cases both the torus model and the starburst contributions were strongly constrained.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Circinus as one of nine disk galaxies hosting pseudobulges and places it among the pseudobulges lying below the paper's inferred scaling relations.

quotes.quote: By means of the S e rsic index, we identify nine disk galaxies as hosting pseudobulges: Circinus, IC2560, NGC1068, NGC3079, NGC3368, NGC3489, NGC3998, NGC4258, NGC4594.

Figure fg:mbh illustrates that 4 out of 9 pseudobulges (NGC3489, NGC3998, NGC4258, NGC4594) are consistent with the correlation for classical bulges, while the others (CIRCINUS, IC2560, NGC1068, NGC3079, NGC3368) are outliers, at more than 4 below the LINMIX ERR linear regression.
Of these, CIRCINUS, IC2560, NGC1068, and NGC3398 lie below all the \ scaling relations; NGC3368 deviates from , - but not from , while the contrary happens for NGC3998.

sources.summary: The paper reports first HC3N and HNC detections for Circinus and classifies it among the HC3N-luminous or HC3N-rich galaxies in the survey.

quotes.quote: New detections This is not only the first text to put together a survey of all extragalactic HC _3 N emission line data, but it also reports the first HC _3 N detections in six galaxies: Circinus , IC 860 , IRAS 17208-0014 , Maffei 2 , NGC 1068 , and NGC 3079 . Three of the HNC detections are also made in sources without earlier HNC detections: Circinus , IC 860 , and IRAS 17208-0014 . The galaxies thus seen as HC _3 N-luminous or HC _3 N-rich are NGC 4418 , IC 342 , Circinus , M82 , Maffei 2 , Arp 220 , and IRAS 17208-0014 .

sources.summary: The paper finds Circinus Galaxy has the sample's smallest hard-X-ray variability, likely because its spectrum is reflection dominated.

quotes.quote: At the other end of the distribution, the Compton-thick Seyfert 2 Circinus Galaxy and the Seyfert 1 IC 4329A show the smallest amounts of variability ( F_ var 0.13 and F_ var 0.19 , respectively). The low value of F_ var of Circinus Galaxy is very likely related to the reflection-dominated nature of its hard X-ray spectrum. h h An inverse correlation between the variability amplitude in the X-rays and the X-ray luminosity of AGN was found by (Barr et al., 1986)using EXOSAT data. Studying the 1-month binned Swift /BAT light-curves of the 20 brightest objects after 58 months of observations, we found that all the objects in our sample show variability, ranging between F_ var =0.13 and F_ var =0.96 , for Circinus galaxy and Mrk 421, respectively.

sources.summary: The atlas gives Circinus's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: Circinus spiral galaxy, which lies within 10 of the Galactic plane, harbours one of the nearest AGN at 4.2 0.8 Mpc tully_extragalactic_2009 . It is optically classified as a Sy 2 with polarized broad lines oliva_spectropolarimetry_1998 . A nuclear water-maser disc (PA 56 ; greenhill_warped_2003 ) and a recent starburst maiolino_seyfert_1998,sanchez_sinfoni_2006 have been found on parsec-scales, while a one-sided kiloparsec-scale ionization cone is also present in this object (PA 44 ; marconi_prominent_1994,wilson_hubble_2000 ).

sources.summary: The paper uses Circinus as a nearby AGN benchmark, citing its broad-band X-ray modeling, gas/maser black-hole mass, and flat intrinsic Seyfert-nucleus spectrum.

quotes.quote: To reduce the above limitations, we gathered from literature the values of the intrinsic X-ray slopes from detailed models of broad-band data sets (including Integral , I, and/or Suzaku , S, data) of several well studied, nearby objects: NGC 4258 (S, Yamada et al. \ 2009), Circinus (S, Yang et al. \ 2009), Centaurus A (I, Beckmann et al 2011), NGC 4151 (I,S, XMM , dim and bright states; Lubi nski et al 2010) and NGC 5548 (S, Brenneman et al 2012).

Crucially for precision of _ 2-10 , the first three have M estimated directly from gas or maser kinematics; for Circinus we use M=(1.7 0.3) 10^6 M_ (Greenhill et al. \ 2003), for Cen A, M = 9.6^ +2.5 _ 1.8 10^7 M_ (Gnerucci et al. \ 2011), and for NGC 4258, M = 3.6 10^7 (Miyoshi et al. \ 1995; uncertainty not reported).

Interestingly, it roughly agrees with the relation, = -0.09 _ 2-10 + 1.42 , obtained by Gu Cao (2009); a flat, with < 1.7 , spectrum of the intrinsic X-ray emission of the (obscured) Seyfert nucleus in Circinus indicates that it extends up to _ 2-10 0.005 .

sources.summary: The paper says Circinus has reflection-dominated X-ray emission, gamma-ray luminosity above the calorimetric limit, and a precisely determined megamaser black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: Similarly, the X-ray emission from Circinus is reflection dominated; we used results from .
However, Circinus does not agree with these correlations, furthermore, its L_ exceeds the calorimetric limit (where all cosmic rays produced by supernovae interact with interstellar matter), see .
Masses of supermassive black holes in Circinus, NGC 1068, and NGC 4945 are precisely determined by H _2 0 megamaser measurements.

sources.summary: The paper presents Circinus as a nearby gas-rich spiral Seyfert 2 with a wide ionization cone, coronal-line spectra, heavy obscuration, and nuclear-star-formation-heated dust emission.

quotes.quote: Circinus (Seyfert 2 - D_ L 4.2 Mpc - 1" 20.4 pc) is a nearby gas rich spiral, hosting one of the nearest Seyfert 2 nuclei known. The Seyfert 2 nature of Circinus is supported by the optical images showing a spectacular one-sided and wide-angled kpc-scale cone, first revealed by marconi1994 (see also veilleux1997 ), and the optical/IR spectra rich in prominent and narrow coronal lines oliva1994,moorwood1996 . Furthermore, from the X-ray spectral analysis Circinus is a highly obscured AGN, but with strong thermal dust emission dominated by heating from nuclear star formation matt2000 .

sources.summary: The paper cites Circinus as a Seyfert 2 comparison, noting a MIR torus diameter below 4 pc and a molecular gas mass measured within a 9 pc radius.

quotes.quote: For example, using MIR direct imaging, Packham05 and Radomski2008 found for Circinus and Centaurus A that the MIR size of the torus is less than 4 pc (diameter).
We do not have any Sy2 galaxy in common with Hicks09 , but we compare with the two Sy2 galaxies in their sample (Circinus and NGC 1068).
For Circinus using a smaller radius of 9 pc, they found a M _ gas ^ H_2 =1.9 10 ^6 M _ .

sources.summary: Circinus is singled out as the sample's only reverse P-Cygni OH119 case, implying molecular inflow rather than the outflows seen in ordinary P-Cygni profiles.

quotes.quote: Circinus is a unique galaxy in our sample, because it is the only one to show a reverse P-Cygni profile in the nuclear spectrum. A reverse P-Cygni profile indicates that there is an inflow instead of an outflow of molecular gas.
We find that one galaxy (Circinus) has a reverse P-Cygni feature, which indicates a molecular inflow.

sources.summary: The paper uses the Circinus galaxy as an AGN polar-gas benchmark, adopting its MIR torus model and showing that simulated polar-component lines remain detectable.

quotes.quote: The two nearest sources in which this polar component has been confirmed by interferometric studies are NGC-1068 refId01 and the Circinus galaxy refId02 , although such elongated emission has also been observed in several other sources (e.g., refId03, 10.1117/12.2231077 ).

The values of R and are kept consistent in all the simulations and have values of R = 1.756 pc and = 1.235 pc, corresponding to the best fit model of the Circinus galaxy (Andoine et al. submitted), leading to a covering fraction of CF = 0.7 .

When considering 0.1 of extra scattered continuum stemming from the NLR in addition to the new polar gas emission, as well as extra photoionized lines from NGC 3393 and the Circinus galaxy, we find that the spectral lines from the polar component would still be detectable, particularly by future X-ray calorimeters such as those onboard XRISM (Resolve; xrismscienceteam2020science ) and Athena (X-IFU; 2016SPIE.9905E..2FB ) (see Figure fig10.5 ).

sources.summary: The paper uses a radiation-driven fountain model based on the Circinus galaxy to discuss multiphase circumnuclear gas, CO absorption diagnostics, and whether AGN tori resemble Circinus.

quotes.quote: Wada2016 considered the X-ray dominated region (XDR) chemistry with the radiation-driven fountain model for the nearby type 2 AGN, the Circinus galaxy, and they found that the circumnuclear region was filled with multi-phase gases: ionized gas, atomic gas, and molecular gas.
Wada2016 modeled a Compton-thick Seyfert AGN, known as Circinus galaxy.

The radiation-driven fountain model Wada2016 suggested a picture that AGN tori are naturally formed by gas circulation around the AGN, and this picture is consistent with multi-wavelength observations of low luminosity AGNs, such as the Circinus galaxy Wada2018a,Izumi2018,Wada2018b, Buchner2021, Ogawa2021 .

sources.summary: The paper treats Circinus galaxy as a Seyfert MeV gamma-ray source requiring a source-dependent fit, with accretion-disk hard X-rays and uncertain gamma-ray emission.

quotes.quote: The remaining two sources, Circinus galaxy and MSH 15 52, are fitted with a source-dependent model as follows. The hard X-ray emission of Circinus galaxy (a Seyfert galaxy) is reproduced by accretion disk emission, while its gamma-ray emission is unknown hayashida_discovery_2013 . We apply a combination of a Gaussian model in the hard X-ray band and a power-law model from 4FGL ( fig:sed in sec:sources_appendix ).

sources.summary: The paper includes Circinus as an AGN/Seyfert galaxy in the sample, uses it as the example image set, and finds high central CN/HCN-related surface-density behavior near its AGN.

quotes.quote: Our final sample of 9 galaxies (Table table:physical ) is quite heterogeneous, and includes galaxies with a prominent active galactic nucleus (AGN) or Seyfert nucleus (Circinus, NGC1808, NGC3627, NGC 7469), luminous and ultraluminous infrared galaxies (U/LIRGs: IRAS 13120-5453, NGC3256, VV114), galaxies with a starburst nucleus (M83, NGC 1808), and spiral galaxies (NGC3627, NGC3351).
The resulting images and image ratios are shown in Fig. fig:exampleimages for the Circinus galaxy and in Figs. fig:appendixfig10 fig:appendixfig5 in Appendix app:images for the other 8 galaxies.
With the highest spatial resolution in our sample (62 pc), Circinus shows a 35 enhancement in the CN/HCN line ratio in the central pixels impacted by its strong AGN.

sources.summary: The paper reports extended high-ionization gas outflow in the Circinus Galaxy, with coronal-line emission reaching hundreds of parsecs and likely tracing jet-inflated shells.

quotes.quote: We report the first characterization of an extended outflow of high ionized gas in the Circinus Galaxy and in IC 5063 (Figure fig:collage ) by means of the CLs [ Fe vii ] 6087 \ and [ Fe x ] 6375 . In both cases, the [ Fe vii ] emission is located within the ionization cone already detected in the [ O iii ] 5007 \ line and is found to extend up to a distance of 700 pc (Circinus) and 1.2 kpc (IC 5063) from the centre. Correspondingly, the [ Fe x ] 6375 \ emission extends up to 400 pc (Circinus) and 700 pc (IC 5063).

sources.summary: The review describes Circinus as a nearby high-resolution AGN case with a nuclear spiral, circum-nuclear disk interpreted as a molecular torus, and polar outflow.

quotes.quote: Circinus is one of the nearest galaxy, observed down to 2 pc resolution, and shows a nuclear spiral, of 20-30 pc, with inside another circum-nuclear disk, which can be interpreted as the molecular torus. It is self-absorbed in CO(3-2) but not in CO(6-5), nor in the dense gas tracers, such as HCO ^+ . In addition to this cold thin disk, fueling the AGN, there is an outflow in the polar direction, composed of warm dust and ionized gas, cf Fig. fig9 Izumi2018, Tristram2022 .

sources.summary: The dissertation discusses Circinus Galaxy as a bright type-2 AGN with strong IXPE polarization, comparing reflection-based interpretations and type-2 AGN observability.

quotes.quote: The latter was followed by , which modelled the X-ray polarisation specifically in the obscured Circinus Galaxy, using the MC MONACO code linked to radiative hydrodynamic simulations.

Similar obscuration conditions have been discussed in the context of IXPE analysis of the type-2 AGN Circinus Galaxy Ursini2023 , the ULX object SS433 Kaaret2023 and the accreting Be-transient X-ray pulsar LS V +44 17/RX J0440.9+4431 Doroshenko2023 .
Last but not least, the brightest type-2 AGN, the Circinus Galaxy 20.0 3.8 in 2--6 keV at a 68 confidence level, Ursini2023 , was observed in July 2022, showing remarkably high X-ray polarisation.

sources.summary: The review treats the Circinus galaxy as a nearby luminous obscured AGN, noting its high column density, Compton-thick nature, and low Galactic latitude.

quotes.quote: That obscured AGNs are common and need to be included in estimates of accretion power is obvious from the fact that the three nearest AGNs with intrinsic X-ray luminosities above 10^ 40 (NGC 4945, the Circinus galaxy, and Centaurus A) are all highly obscured with N_ H >10^ 23 (Matt et al. 2000). Two (NGC 4945 and Circinus) are even Compton-thick with N_ H >1.5 10^ 24 . This situation has only been slowly appreciated, perhaps due to NGC 4945 appearing as a starburst galaxy at all non-X-ray wavelengths and to the Circinus galaxy lying close to the Galactic plane.

sources.summary: The paper detects Circinus Galaxy in hard X-rays inside the GRO J1411-64 error region, finds its emission rather constant, and rules it out as the MeV source counterpart.

quotes.quote: From the possible counterparts of GRO J1411-64 discussed in Zhang et al.'s paper (2002), only the Circinus Galaxy shows up in this error region as seen by INTEGRAL.
As shown in these light curves, hard X-ray emission from the Circinus Galaxy and 1H1254-690 is rather constant, whereas that from GX 301-2 is highly variable.

In addition, a combination of the best-fit spectrum of the Circinus Galaxy at hard X-rays with the one detected at MeV energies of GRO J1411-64, suggests that this Seyfert galaxy is not the counterpart of the MeV source: its spectral extrapolation is well below the ones at MeV energies for both states.

gmw_00000075.name: ESO 5-4
gmw_00000075.overview: ESO 5-4 is an edge-on spiral galaxy located at a distance of 22.4 Mpc [arXiv:1310.2770]. It hosts a hidden Seyfert 2 nucleus [arXiv:2403.05709, arXiv:1310.2770] with centrally peaked radio emission across its optical disk and asymmetric extraplanar emission that suggests a halo [arXiv:2403.05709].

sources.summary: The paper reports ESO 005-G004 has centrally peaked radio emission across the optical disk, a hidden Seyfert 2 nucleus, asymmetric extraplanar emission suggesting a halo, and no jet trace.

quotes.quote: ESO 005-G004: The radio map of this galaxy, which harbors a hidden Seyfert 2 nucleus Mckernan2010,Garcia-Bernete2016 , reveals an extended emission, centrally peaked, covering the entire optical disk. Two optical point sources are located nearly symmetrically on both sides of the disk center. Up to date, its is not determined if these sources are related or not with the galaxy. We found that an exponential function provided the most accurate fit for the disk, and we also observed highly asymmetric extraplanar emission that could not be fully explained with a single component, suggesting the presence of a halo. No trace of a jet that might be associated with the Seyfert nucleus was found.

sources.summary: The atlas gives ESO 5-4's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: ESO 5-4 is an edge-on spiral galaxy at a distance of D= 22.4 Mpc tully_nearby_1988 hosting a Sy 2 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 .
