It is a member of the nine-month BAT AGN sample and has only recently been discovered as an AGN landi_agn_2007 , which is presumably related to the high nuclear obscuration in this X-ray ``buried" AGN ueda_suzaku_2007 . After first being detected in the MIR with , ESO 5-4 was also observed with /IRAC and IRS.

gmw_00000076.name: ESO 33-2
gmw_00000076.overview: ESO 33-2 is a spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.0181 [arXiv:1310.2770]. It hosts a radio-quiet Seyfert 2 nucleus with emission extending 1.5 by 0.6 kiloparsecs in the north-south direction [arXiv:1310.2770].

sources.summary: The atlas gives ESO 33-2's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: ESO 33-2 is a spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0181 ( D 82.3 Mpc) hosting a radio-quiet Sy 2 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 . The emission is extended by 1.5 0.6 kpc in the north-south directions (PA 5 ; schmitt_hubble_2003 ). After first being detected in the MIR with , ESO 33-2 was observed with /IRAC, IRS and MIPS and appears almost point-like without non-nuclear emission in all corresponding images.

gmw_00000077.name: ESO 103-35
gmw_00000077.overview: ESO 103-35 is an inclined early-type S0/Sa galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0101079, arXiv:1310.2770] with a disturbed post-merger morphology [arXiv:1501.02022]. It hosts a variable Seyfert active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0008295, arXiv:0902.2777] surrounded by a clumpy bipolar extended narrow-line region [arXiv:1501.02022] and a dusty torus [arXiv:1311.4880].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes ESO 103--G35 as a low-scattering Seyfert AGN, constraining its infrared-emitting dust and arguing that host-galactic dust may add absorption beyond the torus.

quotes.quote: Moreover, while a detailed analysis of the IR emission of low-scattering AGN ESO 103--G35 remains inconclusive, we do not find any indication of systematically higher dust covering factors in a sample of low-scattering AGN based on their IR emission. For ESO 103--G35, we constrained the temperature, mass and location of the IR emitting dust which is consistent with expectations for the dusty torus. However, a deep silicate absorption feature probably from much cooler dust suggests an additional screen absorber on larger scales within the host galaxy.

We fitted a gray body spectrum to the peak region and Rayleigh-Jeans tail of the IR SED of ESO 103--G35 by fixing the dust opacity at 100 to _ 100 = 0.4 m^2/kg and leaving the spectral index of the wavelength-dependence of _ ^ and the peak emission temperature T_ peak as free parameters. As a result, we obtained a peak temperature T_ peak = 169 2 K and =0.0 0.1 , and accordingly a dust mass of M_ dust = (1.4 0.4) 10^4 . We can estimate the scale at which the bulk of this mass is located, under the assumption that the IR-emitting dusty gas is identical to the X-ray obscuring material, as 17 pc (M_4/(N_ H ,23 _ 0.01 ))^ 1/2 for a dust mass M_4 = M_ dust /10^4 , a hydrogen column density N_ H ,23 = N_ H /10^ 23 cm ^ 2 , and a dust-to-gas ratio _ 0.01 = /0.01 . For ESO 103--G35 we obtain 13 pc , meaning that the bulk of the IR-emitting dust mass is located on scales commonly associated with the torus.

sources.summary: The paper identifies ESO 103-G35 as a Seyfert 1.9 galaxy with heavily absorbed X-ray spectra, Fe-line emission, possible reflection, and variable continuum and column density.

quotes.quote: ESO 103-G35 The HEAO-1 hard X-ray source 1H 1832-653 has been identified with the galaxy ESO 103-G35 at a redshift z=0.013. Optical spectroscopy revealed a high excitation forbidden line spectrum with weak broad emission line wings and therefore classified this object as a Seyfert-1.9 galaxy (Phillips et al. 1979). obtained the first X-ray spectrum showing a power-law spectrum absorbed by a column of N_H 10^ 23 (Warwick, Pounds Turner 1988). The observations also showed a variation in the column density over a period of 90 days. Smith Done (1996) present observations detecting an Fe line around 6.4 keV with an equivalent width of 350 eV.

Two observations of ESO103-G35 with BeppoSAX separated by a year, from 2 to 60 keV (Akylas et al. 2000), show a variation of the power-law flux by a factor of two, no variation of the Fe line flux while there is no strong evidence for the presence of a reflection component. Comparison of the historical data (Poletta et al. 1996) from HEAO-1 up to ASCA show a variation of the flux of a factor of about four.

sources.summary: The paper identifies ESO 103-G35 as an S0/Sa Seyfert galaxy and reports BeppoSAX spectra requiring an absorbed power law plus Fe K emission, with short-term variability.

quotes.quote: ESO 103-G35 The HEAO A2 hard X-ray source 1H 1832-653 was identified as the S0/Sa type galaxy ESO 103-G35, with a redshift of z=0.013, by Phillips et al. (1979). They classified the AGN as a type 1.9 Seyfert galaxy because of the weakness of H emission line and the presence of a broad H line. EXOSAT observations, showed a steep photon index of =1.92^ +0.34 _ 0.34 absorbed by a high column density of N _H>10^ 23 cm^ 2 , which was found to vary within factor of 2 in a period of 90 days possibly due to motion of material in the Broad Line Region across the line of sight (Warwick, Pounds Turner 1988).

ESO 103-G35 (1996) A poor fit ( 2=240 for 173 dof) is obtained with a single power-law model, emphasizing the importance of an Fe emission line. This is again evident in Fig. eso103 where we plot the absorbed power-law model and residuals. Indeed when we include a Gaussian line the 2 is reduced by 66.

sources.summary: The paper includes ESO 103--G35 in the Seyfert 2 sample, extracts ISGRI spectra from mosaic images, and notes PDS evidence for a poorly constrained high-energy cutoff.

quotes.quote: We present results on approximately one year of INTEGRAL observations of six AGN detected during the regular scans of the Galactic Plane. The sample is composed by five Seyfert 2 objects ( MCG --05--23--16 , NGC 4945 , the Circinus galaxy, NGC 6300 , ESO 103--G35 ) and the radio galaxy Centaurus A. The continuum emission of each of these sources is well represented by a highly absorbed ( N_ H >10^ 22 cm^ 2 ) power law, with average spectral index = 1.9 0.3 .

Because of the faint nature of the sources in the sample, we chose to extract the ISGRI spectra for NGC 4945 , the Circinus galaxy, NGC 6300 and ESO 103--G35 from the count rate and variance mosaic images at the position of the source, which in all cases corresponds to the brightest pixel in the 30--50 keV band. ESO 103--G35 The PDS spectrum of ESO 103--G35 is well modeled by an absorbed power law with high energy cut-off ( ^2_ = 0.3 , 4 d.o.f.), but the values of and E_c are not constrained, spanning at 3 level the ranges 0.5--2 and 20--180 keV, respectively. Cut-offs below 100 keV have been found for MCG--05--23--16 and ESO 103--G35 by PDS, but could not be studied by INTEGRAL because of the short exposure time of those observations.

sources.summary: ESO 103-G35 is described as a disturbed post-merger Seyfert 2 or LINER with red continuum and a clumpy bipolar ENLR extending across the WiFeS field.

quotes.quote: ESO 103-G35: The nuclear emission line spectrum suggests that this object is a very heavily reddened Seyfert 2, or possibly a LINER with vey strong \ and [ O 1 ] . The underlying continuum is also very red. The disturbed morphology of the galaxy suggests that it is a post-merger.

ESO 103-G35: A Seyfert 2 galaxy studied by Gu06 with an extensive clumpy bipolar ENLR with an opening angle of 45 orientated at PA = 25 (roughly in the polar direction of the S0 host galaxy) and extending across the field of view of WiFeS ( 27 arcsec (10kpc). Away from the nucleus the ENLR lines are narrow and dynamically inactive.

sources.summary: The paper treats SWIFT J1838.4-6524 as a Seyfert 2 source whose NuSTAR spectrum requires two narrow Gaussian Fe-line components, with the second line likely from highly ionised iron.

quotes.quote: The validity of this was checked a posteriory; letting the line width free did not improve the fit significantly for any of the considered sources, except for the Seyfert 2 SWIFT J1838.4-6524 . This source was found to feature two narrow lines in its spectrum. We fitted this source's spectrum using two narrow Gaussian lines, one fixed at 6.4 keV and one for which the central energy was let free to be minimised. The best-fit energy of the second line was E = 6.86 0.06 keV . This line most likely corresponds to emission from highly ionised Fe atoms.

sources.summary: The paper reports broadly consistent BeppoSAX fits for ESO 103-G35, but a higher high-energy cutoff than Wilkes et al. found.

quotes.quote: ESO 103-G35: The BeppoSAX data of this source have been analyzed by Wilkes et al. (2000). Even if the models used in our work are slightly different, the basic results are in agreement with these authors. The only noticeable discrepancy is about the high energy cut-off measurements. In the first observation we find E _ C = 84^ +134 _ 33 keV. This is the lowest measured cut-off energy in our sample, but it is significantly higher than that found by Wilkes et al. 2000 (E _ C =29 10 keV).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes ESO103-G035 with compatible BeppoSAX and INTEGRAL observations, confirming an exponential continuum decline below 100 keV and no substantial source-flux change.

quotes.quote: ESO103-G035 For this galaxy two BeppoSAX observations are available but only one, performed on October 3, 1996, is compatible in flux with the INTEGRAL measurement. This BeppoSAX observation has been analysed by b37 and b29 . Even if the models used in these two papers are slightly different, the basic results are in agreement and the only noticeable discrepancy is on the high energy cut-off value: Risaliti locates the power law cut-off at 84 keV, an energy which is higher than that (29 keV) found by b37 . Our results, obtained using the model described in section 4 (see Table 4 and figure eso_cont ) are in agreement with these previous studies and locate the cut-off energy between these two values confirming that in this object the exponential decline in the power law shape occurs below 100 keV. The cross-calibration constant C between BeppoSAX and INTEGRAL is 0.7 0.2 compatible with no substantial change in the source flux.

sources.summary: The paper adopts a published broad-band X-ray model for ESO 103--G35, noting a spectral cutoff, modest variability, and consistency with higher-energy fluxes.

quotes.quote: ESO 103--G35 molina06 analysed the broad-band \ spectrum of this source and located an exponential cut-off to the X-ray PL at E_ cut 70 keV, with no substantial flux change from previous \ observations risaliti02_bright, wilkes01 . This source has shown moderate and fast \ variability on time scales of several months risaliti02_nhvar . Using model realizations in , we find that the PL model of molina06 with =1.78 , = 2 10^ 23 cm ^ 1 and F_ 2-10 =3 10^ 11 cm ^ 2 is consistent with the higher energy 20--100 keV flux when extrapolated, but slightly underestimates (by 30 per cent) the latest 20--40 and 40--100 keV fluxes from bird07 . On the other hand, the \ BAT 14--195 keV luminosity is over-estimated by a factor of 2 .

sources.summary: The atlas gives ESO 103-35's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: ESO 103-35 is an inclined early-type galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0133 ( D 59.5 Mpc) hosting a radio-quiet Sy 2 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 . It belongs to the BAT nine-month AGN sample and is an X-ray ``buried" AGN candidate noguchi_new_2009 . The nucleus is obscured in the UV possibly by crossing dust lanes munoz_marin_atlas_2007 .

gmw_00000078.name: ESO 121-28
gmw_00000078.overview: ESO 121-28 is a spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.0405 [arXiv:1310.2770]. It hosts a Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus and is currently interacting with a compact galaxy to its south [arXiv:1310.2770].

sources.summary: The atlas gives ESO 121-28's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: ESO 121-28 is a spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0405 ( D 187 Mpc), interacting with a compact galaxy to the south. It was only recently discovered to host a Sy 2 nucleus donzelli_spectroscopic_2000 , which remained unstudied until it was detected in the /BAT survey tueller_swift_2008 and became a member of the nine-month BAT AGN sample. After its first but faint MIR detection with , ESO 121-28 was also observed with /IRS.

gmw_00000079.name: ESO 138-1
gmw_00000079.overview: ESO 138-1 is an early-type Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:1310.2770] hosting a coronal-line forest active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2401.06972]. Its central engine is heavily obscured by a clumpy torus with polar dust clouds [arXiv:2306.12500], and it exhibits a compact coronal-line region alongside a nuclear outflow [arXiv:2312.09184].

sources.summary: The paper models ESO138-G001 as line-of-sight Compton-thin but with a Compton-thick average torus, strong reprocessed emission, and prominent Fe-line emission.

quotes.quote: ESO138-G001 This source was marked as a CT-AGN based on the data of \ and , claiming to have log N _ H =25.25 _ 0.31 ^ +* cm ^ 2 . For our analysis, we have used \ and \ observations, both having excellent count statistic ( 45k counts in the 0.5-10 keV and 12.7k counts in the 3-50 keV band, respectively). The cross-calibration ratio of the detector on for the source is 1.15 . The fit is worse (reduced ^2 1.33-1.45 ; see Table Table:best-fit_ESO138-G001 and Figure fig:ESO138-G001 ) than those measured in the other sources. The models are better fitted in the soft X-rays when adding all the emission lines listed in Table Table:Lines_ESO138-G001 , following the previous works of .

All three models are almost consistent with each other. Studying all the models, the l.o.s. column density N _ H,l.o.s. =(0.30-0.40) 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 shows Compton-Thin clouds, differing from the results of . In comparison with the average column density of torus, the decoupled \ (Face-On) and \ estimates N _ H,tor =(2.45-10.43) 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 , supporting a CT average column density scenario.

sources.summary: The paper maps the inner 600 pc of Seyfert 2 ESO 138-G001, finding a compact AGN-powered coronal-line region, nuclear outflow evidence, and a photoionised SE blob.

quotes.quote: We study in detail the inner 600 pc of the Seyfert 2 galaxy ESO 138-G001 by means of the Soar Integral Field Spectrograph (SIFS) attached to the SOAR telescope. This source is known for displaying a very rich coronal line spectrum and a blob of high-excitation emission 3 SE of the active galactic nucleus (AGN). The nature of this emission has not been fully understood yet. The excellent spatial and spectral resolution of SIFS allows us to confirm that the bulk of the coronal line forest emission region is very compact, of 0.8 in diameter, centred on the AGN and most likely powered by radiation from the AGN. In addition, evidence of a nuclear outflow, restricted to the inner 1 centred at the nucleus is found based on the detection of broad components in the most important emission lines.

The spectrum of the SE blob is dominated by emission lines of low- to mid-ionisation, with no hints of coronal lines. Our results show that it represents gas in the ionisation cone that is photoionised by the filtered central AGN continuum. galaxies: active - galaxies: kinematics and dynamics - methods: observational - techniques: imaging spectroscopy - radiation mechanisms: thermal - radiative transfer The study of individual galaxies by means of Integral Field Unit (IFU) spectrographs has brought an impressive amount of knowledge about the central engine and the inner few hundred parsecs of Active Galactic Nuclei to name a few Muller11, Mazzalay13, DMay18, Bianchin22, riffel22 .

sources.summary: The paper finds ESO 138 G1 is best fit by clumpy torus models, with direct central-engine viewing, high line-of-sight cloud number, and polar dust clouds.

quotes.quote: The main results of the comparison between each parameter using the gonzalez/2019 as a reference, can be summarized as follows: The viewing angle, i : The five objects best fitted in this work with skirtor display torus inclination angle i between 10 and 50 deg. In the gonzalez/2019 distribution the least frequent values of i are 20 and 30 deg, which are the values obtained for SDSS J164+43 and III ZW 77, respectively. Moreover, the AGN that is best-fit with clumpy (ESO 138 G1), has an i value of 0 deg. It is located in the second largest peak of the distribution in gonzalez/2019 . The number of dusty clouds in the direction of the line of sight, N_ 0 : In the only AGN that was fit with clumpy (ESO 138 G1) the value of N_ 0 =14 is close to the highest peak of the gonzalez/2019 distribution.

For ESO 138 G1, the viewing angle points out that we are looking directly to the central engine. However, the number of clouds in the line of sight is high (N _ 0 = 14 1). This would heavily obscure the central source, explaining its Type II-type appearance.
For the source best fitted with clumpy (ESO 138 G1), we found the presence of dust clouds in the polar region.

sources.summary: The paper identifies ESO 138-G01 as an outlying Seyfert 2 with high ratios, density-linked gas stratification, strong coronal emission, and CLIFF AGN classification.

quotes.quote: The three outliers The three Seyfert 2s labeled outliers (yellow filled dots in Fig. fig:fig4 ) correspond to the galaxies ESO 138-G01, Mrk 1210 and NGC 4507. Although the densities inferred from the \ doublet are comparable to the other sample objects, their \ values are significantly higher than the average of the other nuclei. They depart from the averaged blue dots position in Fig. fig:fig4 a by more than seven times the cluster dispersion in .

With respect to ESO 138-G01, the detailed analysis of ABBP reveals a strong stratification of the emitting gas clouds that correlates with the density as well as with the presence of high ionization Fe species. Mrk 1210 presents a very rich coronal line spectrum that includes [ Si X ] , which is a very high excitation line not frequently detected Ma07 . ESO 138-G01 is also a strong coronal line emitter. It was classified as a "coronal-line forest active galactic nuclei" (CLIFF AGNs), which are characterized by strong very high ionization lines, in contrast to what is found in most AGNs CC21 .

sources.summary: ESO 138--G1 is discussed as a poorly constrained Compton-thick source; the authors combine X-ray and optical-line estimates and adopt a mean intrinsic luminosity.

quotes.quote: ESO 138--G1 collingebrandt00 found evidence for Compton-thick obscuration in this source, based on their \ spectral analysis and comparison with \ optical emission line flux, but the reflection fraction and obscuring column were not well constrained. Suppose we assume that the source PL ( =2.1 ) is mildly Compton-thick with = 2 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 , reflection R =1 with a typical high energy exponential cut-off at 200 keV. Then, normalizing the model to recent \ 20--40 keV fluxes from bird07 leads to a good match to reported measurements up to 100 keV bassani06 . In this case, log L_ 2-10 =42.9 intrinsically, including mild correction for multiple Compton down-scattering wf . On the other hand, using the Sy 1 relationship between the \ and 2--10 keV fluxes described by collingebrandt00 , a higher log L_ 2-10 =43.10 is implied.

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 138-G01 as a type 2 Seyfert with jet-like radio morphology, compact hard X-ray emission, Compton-thick candidate status, and no X-ray variations.

quotes.quote: ESO 138-G01 optically classified this galaxy as a type 2 Seyfert. It shows a jet-like morphology at radio frequencies morganti1999 . The XMM -Newton image shows a compact source at hard X-ray energies (see Appendix multiimages ). It was classified as a Compton thick candidate collinge2000 . This galaxy was observed three times with XMM -Newton in 2007 and 2013.

sources.summary: The paper groups ESO 138-G1 with Sy2 sources lacking absorption variability, favoring smooth gas and clumpy dust in a geometrically thick torus viewed at large inclination.

quotes.quote: Panel 6 of Figure fig:cartoon_Sy2 (e.g. ESO 138-G1, PKS 2356-61, and NGC 788): Sy2 sources without absorption variability, large covering factors Large covering factor is found in PKS 2356-61 if it is fitted to clumpy torus at mid-IR, although the smooth model is equally good from the statistical point of view. , and intermediate or high half-opening angles of both gas and dusty structures. The gas is smoothly distributed while the dust is located within clumps. A geometrically-thick torus of dust and gas is able to explain the observed characteristics of these three objects, where the dust is located in clumps while the gas is smoothly distributed. Interestingly these objects are seen at relatively large inclination angles but, in spite of that, the large value of the half opening angle of the torus prevents a direct view of the accretion disk.

sources.summary: The atlas gives ESO 138-1's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: ESO 138-1 is an early-type galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0091 ( D 38.6 Mpc) hosting a radio-quiet Sy 2 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 . It features possibly elongated radio emission on scales of 0.5 kpc along the north-east axis (PA 35 ; morganti_radio_1999 ), while the NLR has a complex morphology with jet-like extended emission into the north-western direction on kiloparsec-scales (PA 95 ; schmitt_anisotropic_1995 ) and another extended emission feature 1 0.2 kpc towards the south-east (PA 135 ; ferruit_hubble_2000 ). After first being detected in the MIR with , ESO 138-1 was followed up with /IRAC, IRS and MIPS observations.

gmw_00000080.name: ESO 141-55
gmw_00000080.overview: ESO 141-55 is a face-on spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.0371 [arXiv:1310.2770]. It hosts a Seyfert 1.2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1310.2770] that exhibits a soft X-ray excess [arXiv:2007.14742] and a narrow iron emission line [arXiv:2007.14742].

sources.summary: The paper studies broadband X-ray spectra of the Seyfert 1 ESO 141--G055, finding soft excess, narrow Fe emission, a Compton hump, maximal-spin fits, and a Compton-thick reflector.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage 2020 We have extensively studied the broadband X-ray spectra of the source ESO 141--G055 using all available and observations. We detect a prominent soft excess below 2 , a narrow Fe line and a Compton hump ( >10 ). The origin of the soft excess is still debated.

The soft X-ray excess is equally well described by both the models: the blurred reflection from ionized disk and the intrinsic thermal comptonization of disk photons. Both these models require a maximally spinning black hole to describe the soft excess. We found the presence of a narrow Fe emission line at 6.4 with an equivalent width of 55-70 , that is typical of nearby Seyfert galaxies. The best-fitting reflection column density of the MYTorus model is found to be 0.52 10^ 24 cm^ 2 which indicates that the neutral Fe K_ emission line of ESO 141--G055 has originated from neutral Compton thick reflector (possibly the torus).

sources.summary: The atlas gives ESO 141-55's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: ESO 141-55 is a rather face-on spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0371 ( D 156 Mpc) with a Sy 1.2 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 . The first photometric N band measurements of ESO 141-55 were performed by with the ESO infrared photometer system at the 3 m telescope. Later, it was observed with /ISOCAM and /IRAC and IRS.

gmw_00000081.name: ESO 198-24
gmw_00000081.overview: ESO 198-24 is an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1310.2770] that hosts a Seyfert 1 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1311.5676, arXiv:1310.2770]. X-ray observations of the source reveal a primary power-law continuum [arXiv:1311.5676, arXiv:astro-ph/0310248], variable reflection strength [arXiv:astro-ph/0310248], and iron emission lines [arXiv:1311.5676, arXiv:astro-ph/0310248].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes ESO 198--G24 as a Seyfert 1 AGN with soft X-ray excess, Fe K features, neutral reflection, and variability favoring a blurred-reflection disk scenario.

quotes.quote: We have performed a detailed analysis of a long XMM-Newton observation of ESO198--G24. The main results are summarised below: The 0.3-10 continuum is well described by a powerlaw of slope = 1.82 , a soft excess component, and a neutral reflection component including FeK emission lines. The soft excess is well described statistically by two physical models, reflionx and optxagnf .

Variability found in the observations 1, 2 and 3 of the source show that the soft-excess flux decreases when the UV flux increases by the similar amount. This observation can be described in the reflionx scenario by a truncated accretion disk whose inner most radius has come closer to the blackhole leading to an increase in the UV flux and subsequently compressing the corona leading to reduction of the powerlaw flux and therefore soft-excess flux. We find that the best-fit inner radius of the accretion disk decreases from R_ in =4.93_ 1.10 ^ +1.12 R_G to R_ in <2.5R_G from observation 2 to 3. Possibly the reflionx+diskbb model describes the soft-excess better for the source ESO 198--G024.

sources.summary: The paper reports two BeppoSAX observations of H0235-525, finding compatible continua, no intrinsic absorption or soft excess, a significant iron line, and changing reflection strength.

quotes.quote: H0235-525 (ESO198-G24) BeppoSAX-NFIs pointed at H0235-525 twice (January and July) during 2001 and found the source in two different states; consequently the two measurements have been analyzed individually. Fitting each observation with our baseline model, we find compatible parameters for the primary continuum: photon index 1.7 and cut-off energy E _c 130 keV (see Table 3). No evidence for intrinsic absorption and/or a soft excess component is present in both data sets (see ratio of figure 1 (a)).

We measure an iron line with high significance ( > 99 confidence level) but at slightly different energies, 6.4 keV in the first observation versus 6.7 keV in the second one. However, when we plot the iron line energy versus normalization for the two measurements, we find that both parameters are compatible within their relative uncertainties. The only parameter which changes with some confidence between the two BeppoSAX observations is the reflection component.

sources.summary: The atlas gives ESO 198-24's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: ESO 198-24 is an elliptical galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0455 ( D 192 Mpc) hosting a Sy 1.0 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 , which is a member of the nine-month BAT AGN sample. It was observed with /IRAC, IRS and MIPS and appears nearly unresolved in the corresponding images. The IRS LR staring-mode spectrum agrees with the IRAC and MIPS photometry well without further scaling and shows strong silicate 10 and 18 m emission with weak PAH features.

gmw_00000082.name: ESO 209-12
gmw_00000082.overview: ESO 209-12 is a highly inclined spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.0405 [arXiv:1310.2770]. It hosts a Seyfert 1.5 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1310.2770] that exhibits light absorption in the hard X-ray band [arXiv:0902.2777].

sources.summary: ESO 209--G12 is discussed as a lightly absorbed hard-X-ray AGN; the authors extrapolate published high-energy measurements to estimate its intrinsic 2-10 keV luminosity.

quotes.quote: ESO 209--G12 sazonov07a state F_ 17-60 \ keV =1.66 10^ 11 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 => log L_ 17-60 =43.75 and < 22 based on their \ and \ analysis. Using \ xspec and assuming an unabsorbed power-law (PL) model with =1.7 and the above normalization also agrees well with L_ 20-100 stated by bassani06 when extrapolated to higher energies. This PL then implies L_ 2-10 =4.45 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 . The model straddles the 20--40 keV luminosities stated by bird06 and bird07 , thus giving a simple uncertainty estimate between these.

sources.summary: The atlas gives ESO 209-12's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: ESO 209-12 is a highly inclined spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0405 ( D 175 Mpc) hosting an AGN with optical Sy 1.5 classification veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 . It was observed with /IRAC and IRS, with which a compact MIR nucleus with extended host galaxy emission was detected. The shortest wavelength part of the IRS LR mapping-mode spectrum is missing, but the other part of the spectrum matches with the IRAC 5.8 and 8.0 m photometry.

gmw_00000083.name: ESO 253-3
gmw_00000083.overview: ESO 253-3 is a spiral galaxy [arXiv:1310.2770] undergoing an active merger [arXiv:2309.15849] and hosting two galactic nuclei separated by approximately 1.4 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2309.15849]. It is classified as a Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2210.15376, arXiv:1310.2770] that exhibits spectral variability between epochs [arXiv:2210.15376].

sources.summary: The paper classifies ESO 253-G003 as a Seyfert 2 with spectral variability; reflection is detected and torus, disk, and PEXMON fits are statistically similar.

quotes.quote: ESO 253-G003 This source is spectroscopically catalogued as a Seyfert 2 in 2006veroncetty . The and observations of this object are separated by 3 days, so we fit the overlapping 3.0--10.0 keV range of both instruments with a simple absorbed power-law model and checked whether they were consistent. This test showed there was spectral variability between both epochs, with a significant change in the slope .

We performed a simultaneous fit of the and /BAT data with PEXMON and significant reflection was detected with R _ f >1.59. Modelling this reflection with borus02 instead of PEXMON results in a marginally Compton thin torus reflector ( (N_ H,refl ) =24.04 _ 23.93 ^ 24.30 ) covering more than 73 of the sky. Fitting the reflector using XILLVER instead of borus02 results in a highly ionized disk with =2.68_ 1.34 ^ 2.84 and R _ f >0.85 .

The PEXMON model shows the lowest _ ^ 2 =0.89 although all models show similar quality fits ( _ ^ 2 =0.905 for borus02 and _ ^ 2 =0.903 for XILLVER ). All the models are. indistinguishable, and they are consistent with a Compton-thin absorber in the line of sight to the primary coronal emission with (N_ H, los ) 23 .

sources.summary: The paper treats ESO 253-G003 as ASASSN-14ko’s merging AGN host, with two galactic nuclei, an observed AGN disc, and constraints from the second nucleus.

quotes.quote: ASASSN-14ko is a periodically flaring active galactic nucleus (AGN) in the galaxy ESO 253-G003. Tucker2021 show that the host galaxy is an onging merger, with two AGNs in separate galactic nuclei separated by about 1.4 kpc; the periodic flares are associated with the north-eastern nucleus.

Alternatively, the star could be interacting with a preexisting, long-lasting disc, whose origin is unrelated to stripping of mass from the star, for example, an AGN accretion disc, which is indeed observationally evident in ESO 253-G003.

If we take r_ ext 1.4 kpc (the distance between the two nuclei in ESO 253-G003), the required mass is unphysically large, m_ ext 10^ 18 M_ Thus the observed acceleration can only be explained if ASSASN-14ko is part of a binary black hole system with a separation of 3 10^3-10^4 au, contained within the NE nucleus of ESO 253-G003 (with the southern nucleus having yet another black hole given its observed AGN).

sources.summary: The atlas gives ESO 253-3's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: ESO 253-3 is a spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0425 ( D 181 Mpc) hosting an AGN with optical Sy 2 classification veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 . It was observed with /IRAC and IRS, where a nucleus elongated to the north-east with faint extended host galaxy emission being detected. The IRS HR staring-mode spectrum does not overlap with the IRAC photometry but displays possible lower flux levels.

gmw_00000084.name: ESO 263-13
gmw_00000084.overview: ESO 263-13 is a face-on spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.0335 [arXiv:1310.2770]. It hosts a Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1310.2770] that exhibits a transmission-dominated X-ray spectrum [arXiv:1005.3253] and a nearly unresolved mid-infrared nucleus [arXiv:1310.2770].

sources.summary: The paper finds ESO263--G13 best fit by a transmission-dominated model, with the sample’s lowest absorption column density, weakly constrained scattering/reflection, and low-statistics soft X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: The Suzaku spectrum of ESO263--G13 is best fitted by a TD model. A RD model fails to fit the data over the entire energy range ( ^2 > 700). The absorption column density is the lowest in the present sample ( 3 10^ 23 ); the scattering fraction and the reflection component intensity are poorly constrained ( f_ scatt < 0.8 and R 0.1--1.2, respectively). The iron K line EW ( 80 eV) is consistent with that expected by transmission through the observed column density. The soft X--ray spectrum is characterized by low counting statistics.

sources.summary: ESO 263--G013 has conflicting soft and hard X-ray constraints; the authors favor a harder broad-band fit with higher intrinsic 2-10 keV luminosity.

quotes.quote: ESO 263--G013 The only good-quality X-ray spectrum published is the \ XRT analysis by landi07 , who find =4 1 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 absorbing a PL with 2.32 . The observed flux is F_ 2-10 =2 10^ 12 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 and they quote an intrinsic L_ 2-10 =4-7 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 (converted to our cosmology). But extending this steep PL to higher energies underpredicts other \ and \ fluxes by factors of 7-10 bird07, ajello06 . Recent \ data (by M. Ajello, priv. comm., and some of us) analysed in conjunction with the \ BAT data are instead fit with a harder photon-index 1.8 , resulting in reasonable agreement (to within a factor of 2 ) with fluxes over 2--100 keV and an intrinsic L_ 2-10 2 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 ; we prefer this latter broad-band analysis.

sources.summary: The atlas gives ESO 263-13's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: ESO 263-13 is a face-on spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0335 ( D 146 Mpc) and hosts a Sy 2 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 . It was observed with /IRAC and IRS. In the IRAC images, a nearly unresolved MIR nucleus with weak spiral-like host galaxy emission was detected.

gmw_00000085.name: ESO 286-19
gmw_00000085.overview: ESO 286-19 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1605.00644, arXiv:2202.04019] in the late stages of a merger [arXiv:1509.08468, arXiv:2306.14843], featuring a single nucleus [arXiv:1112.5170, arXiv:1612.08181] and tidal tails [arXiv:2202.04019]. It hosts a heavily obscured active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1112.5170, arXiv:1310.2770] alongside intense starburst activity [arXiv:1112.5170, arXiv:1605.00644] and a strong molecular outflow [arXiv:1803.11225, arXiv:1612.08181].

sources.summary: AM 2055-425 is an AGN/starburst composite in the ALMA CO/CN sample; its ratio is lower than other AGN-like nuclei, possibly because of limited resolution.

quotes.quote: This paper presents the results of an archival project using ALMA Cycle 0 data to survey the CO/CN ratio in 17 nearby galaxies. We identified a sample of 8 galaxies with distances < 200 Mpc for which both the CN N=1-0 and CO J=1-0 lines were detected. These galaxies range from nearby starbursts (M83, NGC 253) to ultra/luminous infrared galaxies (AM 1300-233, AM 2246-490, AM 2055-425, NGC 3256, VV 114) to an S0 galaxy with an unusual far-infrared excess (NGC 1377). We measure S/N-matched CO/CN line ratios as well as ratios calculated from the total flux detected in each line with ALMA.

AM 2055-425 has been classified as an AGN/starburst composite imanishi2010 . imanishi2017 find that infrared radiative pumping plays a role in the excitation of HCN in AM 2055-425 and observe high-velocity wings in the CO J=3-2 emission indicative of a strong molecular outflow. It has a S/N-matched CO/CN line ratio that is significantly lower than those of NGC 1377 or VV114 east. As the most distant galaxy in our sample, it is possible that we do not have sufficient spatial resolution or sensitivity to see clear evidence of an AGN signature in the CO/CN line ratio.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 20551-4250 as a puzzling source where starburst-like 3.3 micron emission coexists with a steep continuum, 3.4 micron absorption, and multiwavelength AGN evidence.

quotes.quote: IRAS 20551-4250 . The high quality spectrum of this source is the most puzzling in our sample. A strong 3.3 m emission feature (EW=70 nm) suggests a dominance of the starburst contribution. However, a steep and inverted continuum slope ( 2.7 ) and the presence of a 3.4 m absorption feature ( _ 3.4 0.3 ) points to the presence of an AGN dominating the continuum emission. This is a clear example where the simple classification, based on the spectral diagnostics described in the previous section, fails. We will discuss this source further in Section 6, where an improvement of the above indicators will be discussed.

The mid-IR spectrum of IRAS 20551-4250 shows clear indications of the presence of an AGN. Similarly, the X-spectrum is typical of a heavily absorbed AGN-dominated source. The presence of the AGN was also revealed by the analysis of the polarized optical spectrum pernechele . risa03 and mason showed that a detailed investigation of the chemistry of the dust grains responsible of the 3.4 m absorption is possible, based on the analysis of the substructure of the 3.4 m absorption feature.

sources.summary: The paper reviews IRAS 20551 4250 as a nearby merging ULIRG with intense star formation, a heavily obscured AGN, uncertain bolometric AGN contribution, and a poorly characterized circumnuclear medium.

quotes.quote: We present a general review of the current knowledge of 20551 and its circumnuclear environment. This Ultraluminous Infrared Galaxy is one of the most puzzling sources of its class in the nearby Universe: the near-IR spectrum is typical of a galaxy experiencing a very intense starburst, but a highly obscured active nucleus is identified beyond 5 m and possibly dominates the mid-IR energy output of the system. At longer wavelengths star formation is again the main driver of the global spectral shape and features. We interpret all the available IR diagnostics in the framework of simultaneous black hole growth and star formation, and discuss the key properties that make this source an ideal laboratory for the forthcoming James Webb Space Telescope .

20551 \ is a nearby ( z=0.043 ) ULIRG lying in the luminosity range of IR quasars, with L _ IR =4.3 10^ 45 erg/s. It mostly lacks of targeted studies, none the less in literature there are several related measurements among the statistical analyses of the local ULIRG population. 20551 \ is a merging system in a fairly advanced state (Fig fig0 , left panel), characterized by a single nucleus with a prominent tidal tail and a slightly disturbed core, likely caused by a minor merger or strong secular evolution effects.

sources.summary: The paper studies IRAS 20551 4250 with ALMA, describing it as an AGN-hosting ULIRG with starburst activity, a merging tail, and detected vibrationally excited HCN/HNC emission.

quotes.quote: We present the results of ALMA Cycle 2 observations of the ultraluminous infrared galaxy, IRAS 20551 4250, at HCN/HCO ^ + /HNC J=3--2 lines at both vibrational-ground (v=0) and vibrationally excited (v _ 2 =1) levels. This galaxy contains a luminous buried active galactic nucleus (AGN), in addition to starburst activity, and our ALMA Cycle 0 data revealed a tentatively detected vibrationally excited HCN v _ 2 =1f J=4--3 emission line. In our ALMA Cycle 2 data, the HCN/HCO ^ + /HNC J=3--2 emission lines at v=0 are clearly detected. The HCN and HNC v _ 2 =1f J=3--2 emission lines are also detected, but the HCO ^ + v _ 2 =1f J=3--2 emission line is not.

IRAS 20551 4250 is categorized as an ultraluminous infrared galaxy (ULIRG) with infrared (8--1000 m) luminosity L _ IR = 10 ^ 12.0 L _ , and has far-infrared (40--500 m) luminosity L _ FIR = 10 ^ 11.9 L _ (see Table 1 of Imanishi Nakanishi 2013b). It consists of main single nucleus with the signature of a long merging tail in the southern direction duc97,rot04 . It is classified optically as a LINER/HII-region; namely, it has no obvious optical AGN (Seyfert) signature duc97,yua10 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies ESO 286-IG019 as a ULIRG/post-merger galaxy with a compact core, tidal tails, declining NEOWISE light curves, and a red transient candidate.

quotes.quote: Transient in ESO 286-IG019 ESO 286-IG019 (also known as IRAS 20551-4250) has coordinates RA= 20^ h 58^ m 27^ s .818 , Dec= 42^ 38 59 .42 and is located at a distance of 187 13 Mpc. Assuming this distance, the galaxy has log(L _ IR /L _ )= 12.07 and is also classified as a ULIRG. The galaxy displays a compact core and tidal tails, one of which is very prominent Johansson1991 and is classified as a post merger by Haan2011 .

The NEOWISE light curves for this galaxy show a steady decline in both bands from the first observations until the most recent pair of observations in 2021, in which the measurements in both bands have levelled out. The galaxy only passes our transient detection limit in the W2 band, while L_ W1 =10^ 42.5 erg s ^ 1 , which is significantly less than required to pass the cut. If we take the mean of the final two observations to represent the quiescent host galaxy flux, the transient is much redder than the other transients in the sample. Although there appears to be a levelling out of the decline in the most recent data, we cannot be certain that our last data represents the quiescent host galaxy flux, so it is difficult to determine if the colour is physical or due to an incorrect subtraction of the host flux.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS20551-4250 as a deep-diving (U)LIRG with depressed O/H relative to its mass, high SFR, late merger stage, and solar-like N/O.

quotes.quote: Interestingly, four galaxies namely Haro11, IRAS12112+0305, IRAS20551-4250 and IRAS23128-5919 strongly deviate from the MZR showing metallicities lower by a factor of 2 than expected for these deep-diving (U)LIRGs stellar masses according to the MZR. While Haro11 is not a ULIRG galaxy like the others, this star-forming galaxy is reported to be a well known merger dominated galaxy that shared many of properties observed in ULIRGs. Notably, IRAS12112+0305 was previously reported to have a super-solar abundance ( 2Z _ ) , while IRAS20551-4250 and IRAS23128-5919 where reported to have abundances close to the values expected from the MZR ar their masses . These high estimations of O/H are driven by the nearly solar N/O values obtained here. However, our O/H estimation -- independent of N/O-- is significantly lower, thus suggesting a more complex mixing process of the gas affecting their chemical composition.

We show in Figure fig:MZR that these deep-diving (U)LIRGs have the highest SFR values in our sample ( >100 M _ yr ^ 1 ) and they are in the final stages of their merges. Additionally, we find that the N/O ratios for these four galaxies are similar to the solar ratio, which suggests that they have reached a relatively mature stage of their chemical enrichment history .

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 20551-4250 as an early-stage merging system with an obscured AGN, H II/starburst-like mid-infrared properties, and uncertain but likely significant AGN bolometric contribution.

quotes.quote: IRAS 20551-4250 IRAS 20551-4250 is a merging system in an early stage (Franceschini et al. 2003). It is optically classified as a H II region (Veilleux et al. 1999), while in the mid-IR it resembles a SB galaxy (Genzel et al. 1998). The X-rays emission is clearly dominated by an obscured AGN, with luminosity L _ 2-10 keV 7.0 10^ 42 erg s^ 1 and column density N _H 8 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 (Franceschini et al. 2003). As in the case of IRAS 19254-7245, the relative AGN contribution to the bolometric luminosity is uncertain, but probably significant.

In our sample we can distinguish nuclei with an unobscured AGN (low EW_ 3.3 , flat continuum and absence of strong absorption) in IRAS 05189-2524, IRAS 23060+0505 and the brighter nucleus of NGC 6240, and sources with a heavily absorbed AGN (significant EW_ 3.3 , steep inverted continuum with strong dust and gas absorptions) in IRAS 19254-7245, IRAS 20551-4250 and the fainter nucleus of NGC 6240. The detection of PAH emission line points to the presence of starburst activity in all sources.

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 20551 4250 as an advanced single-nucleus merger with embedded AGN/starburst evidence, strong far-infrared OH65 absorption, and two outflowing components exceeding supplied momentum.

quotes.quote: IRAS 20551 4250 : This is an advanced merger with a single nucleus that shows evidence for both a deeply embedded AGN and a starburst from hard X-rays to mid-IR emission fra03,haa11,gen98,san12 . The relative contribution from the AGN and the starburst based on mid-IR line diagnostics may be misleading, as [ Ne ii ] and [ O iv ] are not detected but the equivalent width of the PAH 6.2 m feature is low, suggesting high extinction far07 . The strong OH65 absorption indeed indicates that the source is optically thick even at far-IR wavelengths (GA15). estimated a 26 contribution by the AGN, while estimated 57 from the f_ 15 /f_ 30 flux density ratio.

OH65 shows a blueshifted line wing up to 400 , while the rest of the doublets show absorption up to 800 . These are best matched with two outflowing components, that provide a total momentum flux of 4.5 10^ 35 dyn, 15 in excess of the combined momentum rate supplied by the AGN and the starburst.

sources.summary: The paper describes this late-stage merger as having ambiguous AGN evidence, post-starburst and composite LINER optical spectra, optical shock excitation, and possible outflow absorption.

quotes.quote: IRAS F20551-4250 (ESO 286-IG019) This late stage merger shows ambiguous AGN signatures. The Chandra X-ray data show extended soft emission inconsistent with AGN Ptak03,Grimes05,Iwasawa09,Iwasawa11 , but Franceschini03 classify the galaxy as an AGN based on the XMM data. There is no [ Ne V ] detection in the mid-IR, though the 6.2 PAH eqw is very low (0.1 ) more consistent with AGN emission Petric11,Stierwalt13 . Imanishi10,Sani08,Nardini08 also claim signs of a heavily obscured AGN with IR data. Our IFS data are dominated by a post-starburst spectrum and composite LINER emission, with the emission line velocity dispersion distribution centered around 100 and trailing to 300 .

sources.summary: The paper treats IRAS 20551 4250 as an AGN-important LIRG with unpublished LL2 spectra and compact emission whose surface brightness barely exceeds the starburst threshold.

quotes.quote: However, to our knowledge, the flux-calibrated Spitzer IRS LL2 spectra have not been published for IRAS 20551 4250 and VV 114 E, although LL2 observations have been performed according to the Spitzer observing log. Nearby Sources with Energetically Important AGNs The following five sources at z < 0.1 are diagnosed to be AGN-important through infrared 2.5--35 m spectroscopy: NGC 1377, IRAS 08572+3915, IRAS 15250+3609, Superantennae, and IRAS 20551 4250 (Table 1, column 10). For Superantennae, IRAS 20551 4250, and NGC 1377, the lower limits of the emission surface brightnesses only barely exceed 10 ^ 13 L _ kpc ^ 2 . As Superantennae is classified optically as a Seyfert 2 (Table 1), the dust covering factor around that AGN could be smaller than the other buried AGN candidates (IRAS 20551 4250 and NGC 1377), so that the intrinsic emission surface brightness could be higher than our estimate in Table 6.

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 286--IG19 as a likely late-stage merger, citing imaging with two tidal tails and a single r1/4-like nucleus plus a Keel-Wu dynamical stage estimate.

quotes.quote: ESO 286--IG19 Imaging of ESO286--IG19 by Johansson (1991) reveals two tidal tails and a single ^ 1/4 like nucleus. This would tend to suggest a late stage merger. Comparison with the sequence of Keel Wu (1995) suggests a dynamical stage of 1.5 (i.e 6 10^ 8 yrs). The radio contours at 4.79 GHz overlayed on the optical image of ESO 286--IG19 are shown in Figure fig_eso286 .

sources.summary: The paper reports faint, low-significance H-line polarized flux in IRAS 20551--4250/ESO 286-IG19, with an unresolved complex feature similar to previous results.

quotes.quote: IRAS 20551--4250 . A detailed description of the optical properties of ESO 286-IG19 is given by Johansson (1991). We find very faint polarized flux in correspondance of the H line at 1 level, with low significance and with a complex structure. The polarized feature is not spectrally resolved (see Table 2). Very similar results on this object has been obtained by Lumsden and his collaborators (2002).

sources.summary: The atlas gives ESO 286-19's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: ESO 286-19 is a spiral-like merger system at a redshift of z= 0.043 ( D 180 Mpc) classified as an ULIRG with an H II optical nucleus johansson_extremely_1991,kim_optical_1995,veilleux_optical_1995 . However, multiple lines of evidence suggest that ESO 286-19 harbours a highly obscured AGN pernechele_spectropolarimetric_2003,risaliti_unveiling_2006,brightman_xmm-newton_2011,imanishi_subaru_2011 . In the MIR, it was observed with /IRAC, IRS and MIPS, where it appears as a compact source in all images.

gmw_00000086.name: ESO 297-18
gmw_00000086.overview: ESO 297-18 is a highly inclined spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.0252 [arXiv:1310.2770]. The galaxy hosts a buried Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus that appears as a compact nuclear source surrounded by extended host emission [arXiv:1310.2770].

sources.summary: The atlas gives ESO 297-18's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: ESO 297-18 is a highly inclined spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0252 ( D 103 Mpc) that harbours an AGN with optical Sy 2 classification veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 . This buried AGN ueda_suzaku_2007 belongs to the nine-month BAT AGN sample. It was observed with /IRAC and IRS and appears as a compact nuclear source with extended host emission tracing the inclined spiral in the IRAC 5.8 and 8.0 m images.

gmw_00000087.name: ESO 323-32
gmw_00000087.overview: ESO 323-32 is a spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.0160 [arXiv:1310.2770]. It hosts an active galactic nucleus classified optically as a Seyfert 1.9 [arXiv:0902.2777, arXiv:1310.2770], with X-ray observations preferring a reflection-dominated spectral model [arXiv:1005.3253].

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 323--G32 as the faintest sample source, with acceptable TD and RD fits but a preferred reflection-dominated model, strong iron K emission, and weak soft X-rays.

quotes.quote: Both a RD model ( ^2/d.o.f 79/89 see Table 3) and a TD model ( ^2/d.o.f 83/88) provide an acceptable fit to the broad band spectrum. In the TD fit, the primary hard X--ray continuum 2.0, is obscured by a column density of the order of 1-2 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 . The relative intensity of the transmitted component is about a factor two lower than that of the reflection component. Similarly to ESO 137--G34, the best fit spectral parameters, in particular the column density N_H and the scattering fraction, cannot be constrained. A RD model is thus preferred as the best fit to ESO 323--G32, the faintest source of the sample.

sources.summary: ESO 323--G32 is treated as a Seyfert 1.9 whose sparse hard-X-ray and optical-line measurements give consistent estimates of its 2-10 keV luminosity.

quotes.quote: ESO 323--G32 This source is classified as a Sy 1.9 in the catalogue of veron01 . The only reliable published X-ray measurements happen to be the \ observations of bird07 , who find F_ 20-40 =6.81 10^ 12 and F_ 40-100 =1.51 10^ 11 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 . In the absence of other information, we parametrize this with a simple PL, 1.2 , which extrapolates to F_ 2-10 =3.7 10^ 12 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 , or L_ 2-10 =2.0 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 . One could also predict the 2--10 keV power based on forbidden optical emission line measurements.

sources.summary: The atlas gives ESO 323-32's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: ESO 323-32 is a spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0160 ( D 70.5 Mpc) hosting an AGN with optical Sy 1.9 classification veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 . No data are available for this object. We observed ESO 323-32 with VISIR in 2008 in four narrow N band filters during one night.

gmw_00000088.name: ESO 323-77
gmw_00000088.overview: ESO 323-77 is a nearby spiral galaxy [arXiv:1310.2770] classified as a Seyfert 1.2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2103.07482, arXiv:1310.2770] that exhibits strong PAH emission indicating star formation [arXiv:1310.2770]. Its central region features an equatorially extended hot dust disk [arXiv:2103.07482] and a relativistically broadened iron emission line [arXiv:0710.1043].

sources.summary: The paper resolves hot dust in the Seyfert galaxy ESO 323-G77, finding an equatorially extended, asymmetric structure consistent with a disk and previous reverberation and CAT3D-WIND size estimates.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we utilise the new near-IR interferometer GRAVITY on the Very Large Telescope Interferometer (VLTI) to study a Type 1.2 AGN hosted in the nearby Seyfert galaxy ESO 323-G77. By modelling the squared visibility and closure phase, we find that the hot dust is equatorially extended, consistent with the idea of a disk, and shows signs of asymmetry in the same direction. Furthermore, the data is fully consistent with the hot dust size determined by K band reverberation mapping as well as the predicted size from a CAT3D-WIND model created in previous work using the SED of ESO 323-G77 and observations in the mid-IR from VLTI/MIDI.

After reduction and calibration of the visibility squared (shown in Figure fig:ReverbComp for 2.15 m) and closure phase, we can already draw some conclusions. There is a clear drop in visibility squared of approximately 0.1 from the 60 m to 130 m baseline at all covered wavelengths except for the 1.99 m bin.

sources.summary: The paper reports ESO 323-G077 has a relativistically broadened iron emission line likely from an accretion disk, placing it among polar-scattered Seyfert 1 galaxies with Type I hard-band properties.

quotes.quote: In three of the objects, we find hints of the presence of broad emission line. In the case of ESO 323-G077, we have proved the relativistic broadening of the iron emission line, probably originated in the accretion disc of a Kerr Black Hole (Jim enez-Bail on et al. 2007 in prep.). Fig. fig:eso shows the ESO 323-G077 hard band spectrum and the fitted model evidencing the presence of the broad Fe line. Therefore, in the hard band, the objects studied present properties typical of Seyfert 1 galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 323-G077 as a highly polarized Seyfert 1 whose polarization implies partial obscuration by the torus and a borderline orientation near 45 degrees.

quotes.quote: The inclination estimation of the Seyfert 1 galaxy ESO 323-G077 comes from the optical spectropolarimetric measurement achieved by Schmid2003 , who detected very high levels of linear polarization (up to 7.5 at 3600 ). Within the framework of the unified model, those levels are inconsistent with the polarization degrees produced by an object seen in the polar orientation Marin2012a . Schmid2003 argued that the system must be partially hidden by the dusty torus and tilted by 45 ^ with respect to the observer's line of sight to produce such a high polarization degree.

Spectropolarimetric observations achieved by Schmid2003 showed that, at least, a fraction of the inner region of ESO 323-G077 must be hidden behind the torus horizon, classifying this AGN as a borderline Seyfert 1 galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper says ESO 323-G077 is not well fit by the simplified continuum model; previous analyses find two warm absorbers, cold absorption, and possible clumpy-torus variability.

quotes.quote: ESO 323-G077 Our model does not accurately fit the continuum, possibly due to the omission of the reflection component and a soft excess that is modeled by more than one component. GM2013, JB2008 present a more detailed analysis. Both analysis find 2 warm absorbers as well as cold absorber that affects the power law in the hard band. GM2013 presents the idea that the variability observed in the warm absorber can be due to a clumpy torus or clouds in the BLR and proposes this to be a source of intermediate type between 1 and 2, being observed at an angle of 45^ .

sources.summary: The paper identifies ESO 323-G77 as a nearby Seyfert 1 1LAC AGN whose position correlates with UHECR events and whose X-ray evidence may support accelerator interpretations.

quotes.quote: We identify 7 `` ray loud'' AGNs which are associated with UHECRs within 17^ and are likely candidates for the production sites of UHECRs: Centaurus A, NGC 4945, ESO 323-G77, 4C+04.77, NGC 1218, RX J0008.0+1450 and NGC 253.

Within the distance _1 , 4 PAO events correlate with 3 AGNs: NGC 4945, Centaurus A and 4C+04.77; at _2 , 7 PAO events correlate with 4 AGNs: NGC 4945, Centaurus A, ESO 323-G77 and 4C+04.77; at _3 , 17 events are associated with 7 AGNs: NGC 4945, Centaurus A, ESO 323-G77, 4C+04.77, NGC 253, NGC 1218 and RXJ0008.0+1450.
The three sources Cen A, NGC 4945 and ESO 323-G77 dominate the cosmic ray budget at all angular distances, having many UHECRs clustered near them.

sources.summary: The atlas gives ESO 323-77's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: ESO 323-77 is a spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0150 ( D 66.3 Mpc) with an AGN with optical Sy 1.2 classification veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 that appears partly obscured schmid_spectropolarimetry_2003 . It was observed with /IRAC, IRS and MIPS and appears compact in all images. The IRS LR staring-mode spectrum exhibits weak silicate 10 m absorption and strong PAH emission, which indicates significant star formation.

gmw_00000089.name: ESO 362-18
gmw_00000089.overview: ESO 362-18 is a perturbed spiral galaxy [arXiv:1310.2770, arXiv:1708.02683] hosting a Seyfert 1.5 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2103.17002, arXiv:1310.2770] that exhibits short-timescale spectral variability [arXiv:2103.17002, arXiv:2308.08528]. The galaxy contains numerous H II regions [arXiv:1511.01495, arXiv:1708.02683] and displays a smooth rotational structure [arXiv:1708.02683].

sources.summary: The paper treats MCG 05-13-17 as a nearby Seyfert 1.5 AGN with spectral variability and a rapidly spinning black hole, using 2016 spectra to test soft-excess models and favor reflection.

quotes.quote: The Seyfert 1.5 galaxy \ (a.k.a. MCG 05-13-17 or \ J0501.9-3239) is a nearby AGN z 0.012 ; 2006Bennert . This source has been observed by , , and \ during 2005 - 2010 , displaying a clear short-timescale spectral variability hereafter AG14 2014Agis . It was reported to host a rapidly spinning black hole a_ >0.96 ; 2013Walton , with a mass of ( 4.5 1.5) 10^ 7 M_ (AG14).

We have performed spectral fits to \ ( 121 ks ) and \ ( 102 ks ) simultaneous observations of the Seyfert 1.5 galaxy observed in 2016. The spectrum presents an obvious soft excess, the X-ray reprocessing features from an illuminated accretion disk, and a primary non-thermal X-ray continuum. We have studied the origin of the soft excess by fitting either with a warm and optically thick corona, with a relativistically blurred high-density reflection model, or their combination. By investigating the variability during the observations, we find that any spectral variability is subtle at best, although there is a hint that the soft excess is less variable than the power-law continuum. Although the warm corona and relativistic reflection explanations cannot be uniquely distinguished from statistics, the model fits presented here and the physical conditions relevant for \ (i.e. its moderately low accretion rate) may argue in favour of the reflection-dominated scenario.

sources.summary: The paper describes SWIFT J0501.9--3239 as a local active galaxy in its bare-AGN sample and analyzes XMM-Newton/Swift X-ray spectra, including continuum, soft-excess, and spectral-fitting behavior.

quotes.quote: SWIFT J0501.9--3239 (also known as ESO 362-G18 or MCG 05-13-17) is a nearby (z=0.0124) Seyfert AGN which is popular for its short-timescale spectral variability Agis2014 . It was reported that this source hosts an extremely spinning supermassive black hole with a mass of 4.5 10^7 M _ Agis2014 . It was also reported that the X-ray emission region is located within 50 r_g (where r_g=GM_ BH /^2 ) Agis2014 . For this work, we used data from the XMM-Newton and Swift /XRT observations of the source. The details of the observations are given in Table table:obs_log and the data reduction procedure is discussed in Section sec:data reduction .

sources.summary: ESO 362-18 contributed 91 HII regions; among the six galaxies it had the highest average SFR/SFR density, the lowest sSFR, and the lowest measured nearest-SN HII oxygen abundance.

quotes.quote: After these cuts, the number of Hii regions that we are left with is: IC 1158 (292), NGC 6754 (423), ESO 362-18 (91), NGC 7742 (323), NGC 7469 (169), NGC 2906 (241).

The average SFR values range from -3.60 ( ^ +0.41 _ 0.34 ) dex yr ^ 1 for IC 1158 to -2.26 ( ^ +0.44 _ 0.31 ) dex yr ^ 1 for ESO 362-18, while for the SFR goes from -2.37 ( ^ +0.27 _ 0.28 ) dex yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 for IC 1158 to -1.29 ( ^ +0.40 _ 0.28 ) dex yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 for ESO 362-18. The lowest sSFR is -10.28 ( ^ +0.27 _ 0.28 ) yr ^ 1 for ESO 362-18, and the highest is -8.16 ( ^ +0.38 _ 0.53 ) yr ^ 1 for NGC 7742.
SN II 2010jr in ESO 362-18 is the object with the lowest nearest Hii oxygen abundance, 8.299 (0.012), while SN Ia 2000do in NGC 6754 is the one with the highest local metallicity 8.630 (0.018).

sources.summary: The atlas treats MCG-5-13-17 as an individually observed active or starburst galaxy, summarizing its prior classification and near-infrared continuum, emission-line, and absorption properties.

quotes.quote: MCG-5-13-17 . This Seyfert 1 is a strongly perturbed galaxy, with the strongest [ O iii ] emission concentrated in the nucleus and showing an extension to the southeast, suggestive of a conical morphology. The optical spectrum is dominated by broad permitted lines and narrow permitted and forbidden lines ara00 . Our NIR spectroscopy is the first one carried out on this source. In J , broad H i and He i lines are strong, with an FWHM of 4500 \ and 5400 , respectively.

sources.summary: The atlas gives ESO 362-18's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: ESO 362-18 is a perturbed spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0124 ( D 52.1 Mpc) hosting a Sy 1.5 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 with an extended narrow-line region fraquelli_extended_2000 , which is a member of the nine-month BAT AGN sample. It was observed with Palomar 5 m/MIRLIN in 1999 in a broad N band filter with a marginally resolved MIR nucleus being detected gorjian_10_2004 . ESO 362-18 was also observed with /IRAC, IRS and MIPS.

sources.summary: The S7 notes describe ESO 362-G18 as a spiral galaxy with smooth rotation, a velocity-broadened Seyfert 2 narrow-line region, surrounding HII complexes, and evidence for a Seyfert 1 nucleus.

quotes.quote: ESO 362-G18: A somewhat extended velocity-broadened Sy 2 ENLR (imaged with HST by Schmitt03 ) is seen surrounded by complexes of \ regions and with other \ regions visible across the field. The galaxy has a spiral structure with smooth rotation. In \ the presence of a Sy 1 nucleus is evident.

sources.summary: The paper models Swift J0501.9--3239 as a Seyfert galaxy with a central supermassive black hole and finds the Kerr solution is not recovered at 3 sigma.

quotes.quote: We apply our model to fit Suzaku data of four Seyfert galaxies (Ton S180, Ark 120, 1H0419--577, and Swift J0501.9--3239). These galaxies host at the center supermassive black holes that were previously interpreted as near-extremal Kerr black holes. For Ton S180 and 1H0419--577, our measurements are still consistent with the Kerr hypothesis. For Ark 120 and Swift J0501.9--3239, the Kerr solution is not recovered at 3- .

gmw_00000090.name: ESO 416-2
gmw_00000090.overview: ESO 416-2 is a radio-loud spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.0591 [arXiv:1310.2770]. It hosts a Seyfert 1.9 active galactic nucleus, which was weakly detected as a compact source in mid-infrared observations [arXiv:1310.2770].

sources.summary: The atlas gives ESO 416-2's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: ESO 416-2 is a radio-loud spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0591 ( D 251 Mpc) hosting a Sy 1.9 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 that belongs to the nine-month BAT AGN sample. Our VISIR observations in three narrow N band filters during one night in 2009 are the first MIR observations of ESO 416-2. A compact nucleus was weakly detected in all images.

gmw_00000091.name: ESO 420-13
gmw_00000091.overview: ESO 420-13 is an infrared-luminous, early-type galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.0119 [arXiv:1310.2770]. It hosts an active galactic nucleus and a strong starburst, leading to its classification as an AGN-starburst composite [arXiv:1310.2770].

sources.summary: The atlas gives ESO 420-13's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: ESO 420-13 is an infrared-luminous early-type galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0119 ( D 48.6 Mpc) hosting an AGN and a strong starburst. The AGN is optically classified either as Sy 2 veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 of an H II nucleus yuan_role_2010 . Therefore, we treat this object as AGN/starburst composite.

gmw_00000092.name: ESO 428-G014
gmw_00000092.overview: ESO 428-G014 is a Seyfert 2 spiral galaxy located approximately 23 to 26 Mpc away [arXiv:1403.4386, arXiv:2404.16100]. It hosts a Compton-thick active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2404.16100] that drives a two-sided radio jet, which interacts with the surrounding interstellar medium to shape the galaxy's gas kinematics and mid-infrared dust emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0509181, arXiv:2404.16100].

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 428-G014 as having extended, collimated [SiVII] coronal emission aligned with its radio jet, suggesting jet-ISM interaction and shock enhancement.

quotes.quote: In ESO 428-G014, the coronal emission is remarkably aligned with the radio-jet (Fig. 2c). The 2 cm emission is stronger in the northwest direction, and [ SiVII ] is stronger in that direction too. H emission is also collimated along the radio structure, but the emission spreads farther from the projected collimation axis and extends out to a much larger radius from the nucleus than the coronal or radio emission (Fig. 2b). Both H and the 2 cm emission resolve into several blobs but the coronal emission is more diffuse.

In ESO 428-G014, the remarkable alignment between [ Si VII ] and the radio emission is a strong indication of the interaction of the radio jet with the ISM. There is spectroscopic evidence of a highly turbulent ISM in this object: asymmetric emission line profiles at each side of the nucleus indicate gas velocities of up to 1400 (Wilson Baldwin 1989). Shocks with those velocities heat the gas to temperatures of > 10^7 K , which will locally produce bremsstrahlung continuum in the UV -- soft X-rays (Contini et al. 2004) necessary to produce coronal lines.

sources.summary: The paper maps ESO 428-G14's inner 300 pc in near-infrared emission lines, finding that the radio jet shapes narrow-line gas kinematics, compresses gas, and excites Fe II/H2 emission.

quotes.quote: Received 1988 December 14; in original form 1988 October 11 firstpage - lastpage 2002 We present two-dimensional (2D) gas kinematics and excitation of the inner 300 pc of the Seyfert galaxy ESO 428-G14 at a sampling of 14 pc ^2 , from near-infrared spectroscopic observations at R 6000 obtained with the Integral Field Unit of the Gemini Near-Infrared Spectrograph. From measurements of fluxes and profiles of the emission lines [ Fe ii ] 1.257 m, Pa , H _2 2.121 m and Br , we construct 2D maps of line intensities and ratios, radial velocities and velocity dispersions. Emission lines ``tomography" is provided by velocity slices obtained across the line profiles, a unique capability of IFUs, which allows the mapping of not only of peak velocities but including also the wings. We compare these maps with a previously published high spatial resolution radio map and find a tight relation between the radio structure and the emission-line flux distributions and kinematics, revealing that the radio-jet plays a fundamental role not only in shaping the NLR but also in the imprint of its kinematics. Blueshifts of up to 400 km s ^ 1 and velocity dispersions of up to 150 km s ^ 1 are observed in association with the radio jet at position angle PA = 129 ^ , which is also the PA of the photometric major axis of the galaxy.

We conclude that the radio jet is launched at a small angle relative to the galactic plane, with the NW side slightly oriented toward us. This angle is small enough for the radio jet to shock and compress the gas in the plane of the galaxy, and for the nuclear continuum to ionize and heat it.

sources.summary: The paper identifies ESO428-G14 as the 26 Mpc Seyfert 2 host of SN 2008fp and analyzes its host-galaxy ISM, including diffuse clouds, atoms, molecules, and DIBs.

quotes.quote: We used optical high-resolution spectroscopy to study the properties of the ISM in the SN 2008fp host galaxy, ESO428-G14. The properties of intervening dust were investigated via spectropolarimetry. The spectra of SN 2008fp reveal a complex of diffuse atomic clouds at radial velocities in line with the systematic velocities of the host galaxy.

SN 2008fp is a normal Type Ia SN ( 2008CBET.1509....1W ; 2011AJ....142..156S ) discovered by 2008CBET.1506....1P in the galaxy ESO428-G14 , which is at 26 Mpc. The reported galaxy type is SABr(0)-pec ( 1991RC3.9.C...0000d ) and Seifert 2.
Atoms, molecules, and DIBs are detected at radial velocities corresponding to that of the Milky Way and the host galaxy, ESO 428-G14.

sources.summary: The paper uses JWST/MIRI to study ESO 428-G14, finding extended polar MIR dust aligned with the radio jet and likely heated by the AGN or jet-driven shocks.

quotes.quote: We present the first JWST/MIRI study aimed at imaging polar dust by zooming onto the heart of ESO 428-G14, part of the GATOS survey of local AGN. We clearly detect extended mid-infrared (MIR) emission within 200 pc from the nucleus. This polar structure is co-linear with a radio jet and lies perpendicular to a molecular gas lane that feeds and obscures the nucleus. The morphology of the MIR structure bears a striking resemblance to that of gas ionised by the AGN in the narrow-line region (NLR).

ESO 428-G14 is a spiral galaxy (see Fig. original_rgb ) with a redshift-independent distance of D 23.2 Mpc (a projected physical scale of 112.5 pc/arcsecond) and a redshift of z = 0.0057 (values extracted from https://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/byname?objname=ESO+428-G14&hconst=67.8&omegam=0.308&omegav=0.692&wmap=4&corr_z=1 NED ). It is classified optically as a Seyfert 2 galaxy 2006A&A...455..773V , is Compton-thick in X-rays 1998A&A...338..781M , and has an intrinsic 2-10 keV luminosity L_ AGN = 3.6 10^ 41 erg s^ 1 Levenson+06 , and an estimated BH mass of M_ BH (1–3) 10^ 7 M_ Fabbiano+19 .

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 428-G14 as an S0/a Seyfert 2 galaxy with a two-sided radio jet, aligned ionized emission, bipolar line ratios, and inner gas non-circular motions.

quotes.quote: ESO 428-G14 is an S0/a Seyfert 2 galaxy as classified by deVaucouleurs1991 . The galaxy is characterised by a two-sided radio jet with an \ cone also being localised along the jet Falcke1996 . \ and + \ narrow-band images obtained with the HST show extended emission well aligned with the radio jet with stronger emission to the north-west as observed in the radio. The ratio /( + ) shows a bipolar structure with larger values to the south-east Falcke1996, Falcke1998 . Non-circular motions based on the gas kinematics in the inner kiloparsec of the galaxy was reported by Riffel2006 .

sources.summary: The paper reports low signal-to-noise BeppoSAX data for MGC-05-18-002 but argues its flat spectrum and low X-ray-to-[OIII] ratio likely indicate a Compton-thick cold-reflection source.

quotes.quote: MGC-05-18-002 This source was observed after the failure of one of the three MECS units. Both the reduced effective area and the short integration time are responsible for the low signal--to--noise ratio of its spectrum. In this case we only used a simple power law fit (Table tab_apx ). Although a detailed analysis cannot be performed, both the very flat spectrum (Tab. tab_apx ) and the very low L _X /L _ [ OIII ] ratio (two orders of magnitude lower than the average Sy1, as shown in Fig. fig_xoiii ) strongly suggest that this is another Compton thick source, probably cold reflection dominated, and with N _H>10^ 25 cm ^ 2 because of the lack of detection in the PDS range. Higher signal--to--noise data are required to confirm this interpretation.

sources.summary: The paper treats ESO 428--G014 as a Compton-thick AGN test case, comparing its VISIR 12 micron flux and line-based X-ray luminosities with the mid-IR:X-ray correlation.

quotes.quote: This leaves ESO 428--G014, which was first suggested as being CT from \ observations by maiolino98 . hoenig08_baldwin quote a 12- \ flux of 0.22 Jy => log =42.51 at a distance of 22.3 Mpc. Our mid-IR:X-ray correlation from Eq. eq:correlation then implies log =42.39 0.06 -- the error being a 1- Monte Carlo uncertainty. Comparing with recent determinations of the intrinsic X-ray power based on the Fe K and the optical 5007 emission lines, levenson06 estimate log =42.40--42.81 for ESO 428--G014 (after applying a small distance correction for consistency).

sources.summary: The atlas treats ESO 428-G014 as an individually observed active or starburst galaxy, summarizing its prior classification and near-infrared continuum, emission-line, and absorption properties.

quotes.quote: ESO 428-G014 . As the host of a Seyfert 2 nucleus bjo86 , the NLR of this object has been the subject of investigation because of the many individual, thin strands that are very closely related to the radio jet and that produce a highly complex, yet ordered, structure fal96 . It also displays a two-side jet with a double helix of emission-line gas. In the NIR, ESO 428-G014 has been studied, among others, by vgh97 and rkp03 . The latter authors reported bright, extended (up to 320 pc) [ Fe ii ] , Br , and H _ 2 emission, parallel to the cone.

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO428 G14 as a Seyfert-2 galaxy dominated by a bent one-sided diffuse jet whose radio and emission-line structures closely match.

quotes.quote: ESO428 G14 . A prominent, bended, diffuse one--sided jet dominates the structure of this Seyfert--2 galaxy (Fig. mrk612 , right). From the central component, the jet heads towards the south--east and bends by nearly 90 degrees to the north--east after approximately 4 ^ . With better resolution (but a lack of signal), Falcke et al. (1998) have shown this structure also at 14.9 GHz. Our radio map and the emission--line images of Falcke et al. ( ) demonstrate the striking structural similarity of radio emission and NLR emission (from HST [ O iii ] and H images) in this source.

sources.summary: The atlas gives ESO 428-G014's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: ESO 428-G014 -- MCG-5-18-2 ESO 428-14 is a spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0057 ( D 26 Mpc) close to the Galactic plane. It is hosting a Sy 2 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 and possesses a bended jet-like radio structure ulvestad_radio_1989 aligned with a cone-like extended narrow-line region (PA 50degre ; wilson_0714_1989,falcke_helical_1996 ). ESO 428-14 was observed with /IRAC, IRS and MIPS.

sources.summary: The paper treats ESO428-G14 as a weaker fossil candidate, noting its central weak outflow and red and blue wings both appearing northwest of the nucleus.

quotes.quote: ESO428-G14 has a very weak outflow in the central 700 pc Feruglio2020ApJ . The inset in Fig. 6 in that paper shows that both the red and blue wings of the outflow are observed to the northwest of the nucleus, hinting at some lopsidedness. This may mean that the outflow is a fossil remnant of a more spherical one, but the evidence, in this case, is much weaker than in the previous ones.

gmw_00000093.name: ESO 500-34
gmw_00000093.overview: ESO 500-34 is a spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.0122 [arXiv:1310.2770]. It is classified as an intermediate object between a starburst galaxy and an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0805.3255, arXiv:2207.08929], with optical classifications ranging from a Seyfert 2 to an H II region [arXiv:1310.2770].

sources.summary: ESO500-G034 is described as intermediate between AGN and starburst, with uncertain radio-core evidence and resolved MIR nuclear clumps leading to a mainly starburst-heated classification.

quotes.quote: ESO500-G034 Optical spectra (Hill et al. 1999) classify ESO500-G034 as an intermediate object between AGN and starburst. Kewley et al. (2000) detect a compact radio core which argues for AGN rather than starburst heating of the dust. However the radio core remained unconfirmed in images by Corbett et al. (2002). The astrometric precision of our observation does not allow to locate the radio core in the VISIR image. The MIR image (Fig. ima2.ps ) displays a 3'' 4'' nuclear extension.

sources.summary: The paper models ESO 500-G034 as an AGN/starburst intermediate whose radio SED needs two star-forming components, yielding a turnover, steep synchrotron index, thermal fraction, SFR, dust luminosity, and low AGN fraction.
